田貫湖.access_car: About 40 minutes from the Shin-Tomei Expressway 'Shin-Fuji IC'
田貫湖.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of 'Reverse Fuji' at the serene shores of Lake Tanuki.
田貫湖.desc:
Lake Tanuki, nestled in the expansive Asagiri Plateau, is a renowned natural dam lake famous for its perfect reflections of Mount Fuji. Every year, from early to mid-November, the tranquil lakeside is adorned with vibrant maple and beech trees, creating a stunning display of colors.

The most iconic sight here is the mesmerizing interplay of 'majestic Mount Fuji, Diamond Fuji, and autumn leaves.' Strips of red and yellow foliage stretch alongside the calm waters, and when the crystal-clear surface perfectly mirrors the majestic mountain and the lush colorful forest, the sense of space is vast and awe-inspiring.

The lakeside road is well-maintained, featuring comfortable wooden walkways ideal for leisurely bike rides and camping adventures. Due to the strong winds and cool temperatures in the mornings and evenings, it's essential to bring a warm jacket for outdoor photography. For the best mirror-like views, visit on a clear, windless morning.

香嵐溪.types: 11 varieties including Iroha maple and large maple
香嵐溪.address: 愛知縣豐田市足助町飯盛
香嵐溪.access_train: About 70 minutes by bus from Meitetsu Higashi Okazaki Station
香嵐溪.access_car: About 15 minutes from Sarutobi Green Road 'Chikaraishi IC' (extreme congestion during autumn foliage season)
香嵐溪.desc:
Kōrankei, located in Toyota City’s Asuke Town, is a majestic gorge carved by the flowing waters of the Baba River, renowned as a top destination for autumn foliage in Japan. From mid-November to late November each year, 11 varieties of maple trees, planted by the 11th Zen Master San'ei of Kōjaku-ji and local residents, reach their peak with around 4,000 trees in vibrant color.

The most breathtaking sight is undoubtedly the interplay of the Baba River, the striking vermilion Kōranzaka Bridge, and the thousands of crimson maples. The vivid red suspension bridge spans the clear stream, with a cascade of red and yellow maple leaves below. When the lights illuminate the scene at night, the entire mountain transforms into a golden world, creating a spacious and poetic atmosphere.

As a top destination in the Tokai region, be prepared for severe traffic congestion on surrounding roads during the peak foliage season. There are well-planned walking paths around the Baba River, featuring natural stone paths and historic steps, so wearing comfortable, non-slip shoes is highly recommended. Visiting early in the morning on weekdays is the best way to avoid crowds and capture stunning views.
香嵐溪.tagline: A premier autumn leaf sanctuary in the Tokai region, where the beauty of 4,000 maple trees lights up the landscape.

德川園.address: 愛知縣名古屋市東區德川町1001
德川園.access_train: 10-minute walk from JR Chuo Main Line 'Ozone Station'
德川園.access_car: About 15 minutes from Nagoya Expressway 'Kurokawa IC' or 'Horita IC'
德川園.tagline: Step into the stunning Tokugawa-en, where the beauty of Ryusen Lake and the vibrant autumn leaves await.
德川園.desc:
Tokugawa-en is a beautifully crafted pond-style Japanese garden, created by the second lord of the Owari domain, Tokugawa Mitsutomo, perfectly embodying the grandeur of the Edo period. Each year, from late November to early December, hundreds of vibrant Iroha maple and ginkgo trees reach their peak, adorning this historic site with classical elegance.

The visual highlight is the exquisite dialogue between the classical pond, the wooden Ozone Gate, and the autumn leaves. The colorful foliage lines the gentle gravel paths, and when the tranquil surface of Ryusen Lake perfectly reflects the majestic Kansen Tower and the vibrant colors of the trees, the expansive scene radiates an elegant Eastern Zen atmosphere.

The garden's interior is well-planned, with smooth gravel paths that are very friendly for seniors and strollers. During the autumn leaves festival, the famous dreamlike illumination at night enhances the ethereal reflections. On regular mornings, entering as soon as the gates open allows for stunning captures of the cultural scenery through the historic architecture.

常陸海濱公園.fee: 大人 450 円（紅葉高峰期加收 250 円）
常陸海濱公園.address: 茨城縣ひたちなか市馬渡字大沼605-4
常陸海濱公園.access_train: About 15 minutes by bus from JR 'Katsuta Station'
常陸海濱公園.access_car: Easily accessible from the 'Hitachi Seaside Park IC' on the Hitachinaka Expressway.
常陸海濱公園.tagline: Coral hills ablaze with broom grass, autumn's most enchanting spectacle awaits!
常陸海濱公園.desc: 常陸海濱公園 is renowned for its expansive 3.5 hectares of broom grass (ほうき草) turning vibrant coral red every October. From the viewpoint on Misaki Hill, the sweeping fields resemble a fiery blaze stretching to the horizon. Paired with the blooming cosmos fields, this spot becomes Japan's most talked-about photo destination in autumn. It's best to visit on weekdays to avoid the crowds.

袋田瀑布.fee: 大人 300 円（隧道通行費）
袋田瀑布.address: 茨城縣久慈郡大子町袋田3-19
袋田瀑布.access_train: About 10 minutes by bus from JR 'Fukuroda Station'
袋田瀑布.access_car: Approximately 50 minutes from the Joban Expressway 'Naka IC'
袋田瀑布.tagline: One of Japan's three great waterfalls, framed by vibrant autumn leaves across four cascading tiers.
袋田瀑布.desc: Fukuroda Falls is one of Japan's three famous waterfalls, boasting a magnificent four-tiered cascade. In autumn, the maple leaves on both sides of the valley turn a brilliant red, creating a breathtaking scene where the waterfall and foliage blend seamlessly. From the observation platform, visitors can enjoy the most spectacular autumn view in Ibaraki Prefecture. By traversing the viewing tunnel and climbing to the second observation deck, you can take in this stunning three-dimensional panorama of fall colors from various angles.

足利花卉公園.types: Maple trees, various color-changing plants (famous for nighttime illumination)
足利花卉公園.fee: 大人 800 円～2,200 円（依開花狀況調整）
足利花卉公園.access_train: About a 3-minute walk from JR Ryomo Line "Ashikaga Flower Park Station"
足利花卉公園.access_car: Approximately 20 minutes from the Tohoku Expressway "Sano-Fujioka IC"
足利花卉公園.tagline: Stroll through a horticultural paradise, where hydrangeas and autumn leaves dance under a canopy of vibrant lights.
足利花卉公園.desc:
足利花卉公園 is renowned worldwide for its stunning wisteria in spring, but come early autumn, the park transforms with brilliant foliage and water sweetgrass. Thousands of colorful trees bloom across the expansive greenery, seamlessly transitioning into Japan's top winter illumination, adorning the exquisite European-style gardens with dazzling lights.

The most iconic visual experience is the "Nighttime Dreamy Illumination and the Mirror Reflection of Colorful Foliage." Standing by the serene waterside pond, the illuminated autumn leaves contrast beautifully with the floating lanterns on the water's surface. As a gentle breeze passes, the scene becomes rich in layers, showcasing the magical fantasy style of top-tier horticulture.

The park is meticulously planned with fully accessible, smooth pathways, making it very friendly for seniors and strollers. The best time to visit is around 4 PM, allowing you to capture the vibrant sunlight on the foliage before sunset, and then witness the stunning night view as the lights come on.

高尾山.fee: 免費（纜車大人往返 950 円）
高尾山.address: 東京都八王子市高尾町
高尾山.access_train: Get off at Takaosan-guchi Station on the Keio Takao Line, right at the trailhead
高尾山.access_car: About 5 minutes from the Chuo Expressway 'Takaosan IC' (parking fills up quickly)
高尾山.desc:
Takaosan is a historic mountain beloved by Tokyoites and recognized as a Michelin three-star destination. With its diverse flora, the vibrant maples along the hiking trails and around Yakuoin transform into a stunning display of color from mid to late November, creating a picturesque autumn landscape just outside the city.

The most iconic experience here is riding the Takaosan Cable Car. Passengers can ascend on Japan's steepest cable car, surrounded by walls of brilliant red leaves that create a mesmerizing tunnel of yellow and orange hues.

As one of Tokyo's most accessible attractions, Takaosan can get quite crowded on weekends during the fall foliage season, especially at the popular trailhead and cable car station. The shrine grounds feature well-maintained gravel paths that are easy to walk on. For a chance to capture the serene beauty of the ancient architecture without crowds, it's highly recommended to arrive before 8 AM on weekdays.
高尾山.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of autumn at this Michelin three-star sacred site.

明治神宮外苑銀杏並木.address: 東京都新宿區霞丘町至港區北青山
明治神宮外苑銀杏並木.access_train: About a 5-minute walk from Aoyama-itchome Station or Gaiemmae Station on the subway
明治神宮外苑銀杏並木.access_car: Located in the city center; taking the subway is highly recommended.
明治神宮外苑銀杏並木.tagline: Stroll through a world-class golden avenue, where nature meets art in a breathtaking tunnel of ginkgo trees.
明治神宮外苑銀杏並木.desc:
Meiji Jingu Gaien Ginkgo Avenue is a globally renowned urban tree-lined street, celebrated as one of Japan's top 100 roads. Each year, from late November to early December, the 300-meter-long avenue transforms into a stunning golden spectacle, flanked by 146 towering, cone-shaped ginkgo trees at their peak.  
  
The greatest visual allure lies in the "four rows of trimmed ginkgo trees, the grand avenue, and the majestic backdrop of the Seiji Togo Memorial Sompo Japan Nipponkoa Museum of Art." As you leisurely stroll along the smooth central pathway, the lush golden branches intertwine above, framing the solemn dome of the museum in the distance and showcasing the epitome of refined modern urban aesthetics.  
  
Located in the bustling city center, the spot is easily accessible and very friendly for strollers and wheelchairs. During the peak autumn foliage season, designated weekends see the area transformed into a pedestrian paradise (with vehicle access restricted), making it highly recommended to visit during this time. For the best lighting, arrive early on weekdays before dawn for stunning photo opportunities.

國營昭和紀念公園.count: about 100 ginkgo trees, about 300 maple trees
國營昭和紀念公園.access_train: Just a short walk from JR Ome Line's Nishitachikawa Station
國營昭和紀念公園.access_car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Kunitachi Fuchu IC on the Chuo Expressway
國營昭和紀念公園.tagline: Stroll through Tachikawa's golden avenues, dazzled by a breathtaking carpet of yellow leaves along the canals.
國營昭和紀念公園.desc:
Kokuei Showa Kinen Koen spans an impressive 39 times the size of Tokyo Dome, making it one of Kanto's most iconic autumn destinations. From early to mid-November each year, the park's two grand ginkgo corridors reach their peak, with towering ginkgo trees transforming into a brilliant sea of gold, creating an atmosphere of pure romance.

The highlight is the perfect blend of the Tachikawa Fountain Canal and the golden tunnel. As you stroll along the 200-meter waterway, the neatly arranged ginkgo trees intertwine above, harmonizing with the clear waters and European-style fountains. During the leaf-falling season, the ground is covered in a golden carpet, offering a striking visual impact.

Given the park's vast size, it's highly recommended to rent a bicycle at any of the entrances for easy navigation. The overlap of ginkgo and maple seasons is a peak time of the year, attracting large crowds on weekends. Seasoned photographers strongly suggest visiting on a weekday morning right at opening time for the best chance to capture the stunning landscape without any people.

箱根.hours: 24 小時開放（海賊船與纜車營運至 17:00 左右）
箱根.address: 神奈川縣足柄下郡箱根町
箱根.access_train: Transfer from Odawara Station or Hakone Yumoto Station to the Hakone Mountain Bus or Railway
箱根.access_car: About 20 minutes from Hakone Entrance IC on the Odawara-Atsugi Road
箱根.desc:
Hakone Onsen is a vast resort destination that beautifully blends active volcanic geothermal features, historical sites, and art museums. From mid-November to late November each year, the maple trees and autumn foliage along the Hakone Mountain Railway, Gora Park, and the shores of Lake Ashi transform into a vibrant tapestry of colors, painting the mountains in rich autumn hues.

The core highlight is the stunning harmony of traditional Japanese architecture, Mount Fuji, and the autumn leaves. Standing by Lake Ashi, visitors can capture the majestic peak of Mount Fuji, the sacred 'Water Torii' of Hakone Shrine, and the vibrant red maple leaves swaying at the water's edge, creating a breathtaking scene that balances vastness and intimacy.

Given Hakone's expansive area and scattered attractions, it is highly recommended to purchase the 'Hakone Free Pass' for easy travel on the pirate ship and aerial tramway. Popular routes during the autumn foliage season can experience heavy traffic, so planning a two-day trip is advisable. Early mornings offer the calmest waters of Lake Ashi, making it the perfect time to capture the flawless reflection of Mount Fuji and the autumn leaves.
箱根.tagline: Ride the Hakone Mountain Railway through hot spring forests, savoring views of Mount Fuji and the vermilion torii by Lake Ashi.

上高地.fee: 免費（需搭乘指定接駁巴士）
上高地.address: 長野縣松本市安曇上高地
上高地.access_train: About 65 minutes by bus from Matsumoto Electric Railway "Shinshimashima Station"
上高地.access_car: Private vehicles are prohibited. Park at "Sawado" or "Hirayu" parking lots and transfer to a bus.
上高地.desc:
Kamikochi is nestled in a highland valley at an elevation of about 1,500 meters, renowned as a special scenic spot known as "the divine path." From mid to late October each year, the larch and birch trees in the park reach their peak, transforming the entire pristine valley into a dazzling golden world, showcasing an ethereal beauty.

The core visual highlight is the classic scene of "the blue clear stream of Azusa River, the peaks of Hotaka, and the golden colored forest." Strolling along the well-maintained, gentle wooden pathways, you can capture the majestic snow-capped peaks behind the iconic Kappa Bridge or photograph the mirror-like spectacle of dead trees and vibrant forests reflected on the tranquil surface of Taisho Pond.

To protect the fragile natural ecosystem, private vehicles are completely prohibited in the park. Visitors must take an eco-friendly bus from designated transfer stations to enter the mountains. While the highland trails are flat and easy to walk, they can be cold and rocky, so wearing hiking shoes is recommended. Arriving on weekday mornings allows you to capture the most ethereal scenes of mist swirling around the landscape.
上高地.tagline: Wander through the divine path of Chubu Sangaku National Park, capturing the clear streams of Azusa River, Taisho Pond, and golden larch trees.

千疊敷窪地.hours: 纜車首班 08:00 左右（視季節變動）
千疊敷窪地.fee: 免費（纜車＋巴士大人往返約 4,000 円）
千疊敷窪地.access_train: About 45 minutes by bus from JR Komagane Station to the cable car station.
千疊敷窪地.access_car: Private cars are not allowed. Park at Sugandai parking lot and transfer to a bus.
千疊敷窪地.tagline: Ride the Central Alps cable car to the sky basin, and be awed by the golden hues of the first light.
千疊敷窪地.desc:
Senjojiki Kar is a magnificent semi-circular basin carved by glaciers over 20,000 years ago, standing at an elevation of 2,612 meters. Due to its altitude and latitude, this area witnesses the earliest autumn foliage in the Kanto region. From early to mid-September, the vast basin's alpine plants begin to change color, transforming into a stunning blanket of gold.

The most breathtaking sight here is the interplay of the rugged walls of Hōken-dake, the massive glacier basin, and the vibrant, colorful carpet of nature. As you ascend slowly on the Central Alps aerial cable car, you can enjoy a bird's-eye view of the sprawling valleys below, where the golden and red hues create a striking visual tapestry.

Given its true alpine terrain, by late September, temperatures at the summit can drop to single digits, and strong winds are common. If you're visiting for the autumn leaves, be sure to wear professional hiking boots and a warm, windproof jacket. A circular walking trail within the basin takes about 40 minutes to complete. Choose a clear morning for your hike to maximize visibility.

白駒之池.count: Native forests and moss above 2,100 meters
白駒之池.address: 長野縣南佐久郡小海町・佐久穂町
白駒之池.access_train: About 60 minutes by bus from JR Chino Station on the Makigusa Tōge Line
白駒之池.access_car: About 30 minutes from Yatsuhotakogen IC on the Chubu Expressway
白駒之池.tagline: Step into the pristine mossy forests of the Northern Yatsugatake and be amazed by Japan's highest natural lake and fiery maples.
白駒之池.desc:
Shirakoma no Ike is nestled within the ancient forests of Northern Yatsugatake, standing at an impressive altitude of 2,115 meters. It is the largest natural lake in Japan above 2,000 meters. From mid-September to early October each year, the maple and birch trees surrounding the lake begin to change color, blanketing the vast plateau in rich early autumn hues.

The core visual highlight is the interplay of "green moss carpets, deep forests, and the vibrant shadows of red maples." Stroll along the well-maintained wooden pathways, flanked by golden foliage hidden among hundreds of rare moss species and towering ancient trees. When the clear water perfectly reflects the colorful blooms, it showcases an ethereal beauty.

The walk from the entrance parking lot to the lakeside takes only about 15 minutes, with the gentle wooden paths making it ideal for a relaxed hike with the whole family. Due to the low temperatures in this high-altitude area, be sure to wear warm, windproof jackets when coming to enjoy the autumn colors. Visiting on weekday mornings allows you to capture the sacred Zen atmosphere in the air.

河口湖（紅葉迴廊）.address: 山梨縣南都留郡富士河口湖町河口
河口湖（紅葉迴廊）.access_train: 20 minutes by Kawaguchiko Sightseeing Bus from Fujikyu Railway "Kawaguchi Station"
河口湖（紅葉迴廊）.tagline: Discover a stunning autumn oasis along Lake Kawaguchi, where a tunnel of vibrant maple trees awaits.
河口湖（紅葉迴廊）.desc:
Kawaguchi Lake is a must-visit destination surrounding Mount Fuji, and the "Momiji Corridor" on the north shore is the highlight of autumn. From early to mid-November, the maple trees lining the banks of the pear river turn a brilliant red, creating a deep crimson tunnel that enhances the breathtaking views of Mount Fuji.

The most stunning sight is undoubtedly the "perfect frame of Mount Fuji, the pear river's reflection, and a vibrant wall of colorful foliage." Stroll along the smooth central pathway, where the lush golden-red branches intertwine above you. When the evening lights turn on, a dreamlike play of shadows dances across the colorful forest, showcasing an exquisite magical aesthetic.

As this is a world-renowned spot, the autumn foliage festival often faces severe traffic congestion and large tourist crowds. It is highly recommended to set out early on weekday mornings before dawn. The pathway is completely flat and accessible, making it very friendly for strollers and wheelchairs. Visiting in the early morning on weekdays allows you to capture the ethereal serenity in the air.

清津峽.count: combination of native forest and artistic tunnel
清津峽.address: 新潟縣十日町市小出癸2119-2
清津峽.access_train: About 25 minutes by bus from JR Echigo-Yuzawa Station
清津峽.access_car: About 25 minutes from the Kanetsu Expressway Echigo-Yuzawa IC
清津峽.tagline: Step into Japan's top three gorge, awed by the stunning light tunnel and V-shaped cliffs adorned in autumn colors.
清津峽.desc:
Kiyotsukyo is a nationally designated scenic spot carved by the Kiyotsu River over the years, ranking alongside Kurobe Gorge and Oosugidani as one of Japan's top three gorges. From late October to early November each year, the towering Japanese beech and maple trees on the steep, majestic columnar cliffs transform into a brilliant canvas of golden hues.

The most breathtaking sight here is the "Tunnel of Light," designed by architectural master Hiroshi Nakamura. Standing at the observation deck at the end of the tunnel, the semi-circular water mirror perfectly reflects the rugged gray rock walls, the emerald stream, and the vibrant autumn foliage, creating a grand tableau of movement and stillness.

The gorge's walking path has been completely renovated into a smooth, safe semi-indoor scenic tunnel, making it easy to stroll through, regardless of the weather. During peak season, to ensure safety, tunnel visits are by reservation only, so be sure to check the official website for the latest schedule before you go. Aim to visit around noon for the best colors of the autumn leaves when sunlight floods the valley.

定山溪.types: Iroha maple, large maple, cherry blossom trees
定山溪.count: surrounding the hot spring town and throughout the valley
定山溪.hours: 24 小時開放（紅葉巴士營運至 15:00 左右）
定山溪.address: 北海道札幌市南區定山溪溫泉
定山溪.access_train: About 60 minutes by bus from JR Sapporo Station to "Jozankei Shako-mae"
定山溪.access_car: Approximately 50 minutes via National Route 230 from downtown Sapporo
定山溪.desc:
Jozankei Onsen, nestled along the upper reaches of the Toyohira River in Sapporo's outskirts, is a quintessential destination for autumn leaf viewing in Hokkaido. From early to mid-October, the pristine forests flanking the hot spring town reach their peak, transforming the lush green valley into a vibrant tapestry of colorful foliage.

The most iconic sight here is the perfect harmony of the "Futami Bridge and the valley's autumn leaves." This striking vermilion bridge spans a clear stream, with a cascade of red and yellow trees beneath it. When the calm waters of the Toyohira River perfectly reflect the cliffs and colorful forests, the scene becomes expansive and deeply poetic.

During peak foliage season, weekends often see a surge of visitors arriving by car and enjoying leisurely walks in the hot spring area. It's best to visit on weekday mornings to secure a spot on the trails. With ample shade and a refreshing atmosphere, this is an ideal location for a rejuvenating day trip to the hot springs. The morning sunlight casting shadows on the stream and leaves creates the perfect conditions for landscape photography.
定山溪.tagline: Discover the breathtaking beauty of Jozankei's autumn foliage and the stunning red Futami Bridge.

北海道大學銀杏並木.hours: 24 小時開放（校園內請保持安靜）
北海道大學銀杏並木.address: 北海道札幌市北區北13條西5～7丁目
北海道大學銀杏並木.access_train: About a 5-minute walk from the Namboku Line "Kitajuhonmachi Station"
北海道大學銀杏並木.access_car: General vehicles are prohibited on campus; please use nearby paid parking.
北海道大學銀杏並木.tagline: Step into Sapporo's academic haven, awed by a 300-meter golden tunnel and a carpet of golden leaves.
北海道大學銀杏並木.desc:
At Hokkaido Daigaku's "Ginkgo Avenue," the peak season from late October to early November transforms the landscape into a dazzling sea of gold. Towering 70 giant ginkgo trees create an enchanting autumn atmosphere, renowned for their vibrant color and breathtaking beauty across the entire region.  
  
The highlight is the stunning 300-meter-long "Golden Tunnel." Stroll along the smooth campus path as the lush golden branches intertwine overhead, immersing you in an endless colorful corridor. As the season progresses, fallen leaves blanket the ground, forming a thick golden carpet that delivers a powerful visual impact.  
  
Located in the heart of Sapporo, this spot is easily accessible and completely free to enter, with well-planned pathways. During weekends in the peak foliage season, the university turns into a pedestrian paradise, making it highly recommended to visit at this time. For the best experience, arrive early on weekdays before dawn to enjoy the ethereal atmosphere and perfect lighting for photography.

奧入瀨溪流.address: 青森縣十和田市大字奧瀨
奧入瀨溪流.access_train: Take a JR bus from JR Hachinohe Station or Aomori Station to Towada Lake, approximately 120 minutes.
奧入瀨溪流.access_car: During peak autumn foliage, traffic restrictions apply, and you must park in designated lots and take a shuttle bus.
奧入瀨溪流.desc:
奥入瀬溪流 is a premier ecological corridor designated as a Special Place of Scenic Beauty and Natural Monument in Japan. Every year from mid to late October, the 14-kilometer stretch of stream transforms into a dazzling spectacle as towering Japanese beech and maple trees burst into vibrant golden hues, showcasing the ultimate beauty of early autumn in the north.

The main attraction lies in the interplay of "rushing waterfalls, primordial moss, and fiery autumn leaves." Strolling along the well-maintained, gentle pathways, you can witness the "Ashura no Nagare" and other swift streams creating frothy white cascades, beautifully contrasting with the colorful foliage among moss-covered rocks and fallen leaves, presenting a refined natural aesthetic.

The area is extensive, and the flat terrain of the trails makes it perfect for a leisurely hike or bike ride. After the rainy season and autumn showers, the moisture enhances the contrast between the moss and the autumn leaves. It's highly recommended to visit during the cool early morning hours, when sunlight filters through the treetops, creating the ideal conditions for stunning photography.
奧入瀨溪流.tagline: Wander through Towada-Hachimantai National Park, immersed in mossy streams and golden beech tunnels.

弘前公園.count: about 1,000 maple trees, 2,600 cherry blossom trees
弘前公園.fee: 大人 310 円（本丸/北之郭區域）
弘前公園.address: 青森縣弘前市下白銀町1
弘前公園.access_train: About 15 minutes by loop bus from JR Hirosaki Station
弘前公園.access_car: About 20 minutes from Tohoku Expressway "Owani Hirosaki IC"
弘前公園.tagline: Experience the stunning autumn hues at Hirosaki Park, where history and nature unite in breathtaking beauty.
弘前公園.desc:
Hirosaki Park, built on the historic site of Hirosaki Castle from the Edo period, is a perfect blend of history and nature. From late October to mid-November each year, the park's 1,000 maple trees and 2,600 cherry blossom trees turn vibrant red, adding a splash of color to the majestic ancient castle walls.

The park's highlight is the classic view of the retro castle moat adorned with autumn leaves. Colorful blooms line the gentle waterways, and when the calm surface reflects the vermilion "Shakyo Bridge," the wooden castle tower preserved since the Edo period, and the vibrant foliage, the scene opens up beautifully, showcasing elegant Japanese aesthetics.

The park's terrain is completely flat, with well-maintained pathways along the moat, making it very accessible for seniors and travelers with strollers. During the autumn foliage festival, enchanting nighttime illuminations enhance the reflections, creating a dreamlike atmosphere. Visiting early in the morning on weekdays offers the least crowds, perfect for capturing stunning mirror-like photos.

八幡平.count: a large-scale primitive forest spanning two prefectures
八幡平.hours: 24 小時開放（冬季封路：11月上旬～4月中旬）
八幡平.address: 岩手縣八幡平市至秋田縣鹿角市
八幡平.access_train: About 120 minutes by bus from JR Morioka Station to Hachimantai Summit
八幡平.access_car: About 20 minutes from the Tohoku Expressway Matsuo Hachimantai IC
八幡平.tagline: Explore the breathtaking landscapes of Towada-Hachimantai National Park along the scenic road, where Onuma's shores meet vibrant autumn foliage.
八幡平.desc:
Hachimantai is a vast shield volcano plateau that spans Iwate and Akita, reaching an elevation of 1,613 meters. Due to its altitude and latitude, high-altitude plants around the volcanic marshes and plains begin to change color from late September to early October, blanketing the plateau in rich early autumn hues.

The core visual highlight is a leisurely drive along the Aspite Line, where golden foliage hides among towering birch and beech trees. The wetlands around the famous Onuma, with their dynamic interplay of light and shadow, perfectly embody the wild beauty of the northern wilderness.

As it is situated at a high altitude, temperatures at the summit can drop to single digits by late September. Be sure to wear a windproof and warm professional jacket when coming to enjoy the autumn leaves and photography. The area around Onuma features a smooth, comfortable wooden boardwalk, and visiting on weekday mornings ensures fewer crowds, making it ideal for a leisurely stroll and ecological photography.

中尊寺.count: approximately hundreds along Tsukimi-zaka and within the grounds
中尊寺.fee: 大人 800 円（金色堂拜觀）
中尊寺.address: 岩手縣西磐井郡平泉町平泉衣關202
中尊寺.access_train: About a 20-minute walk from JR 'Hiraizumi Station' or a 5-minute ride on the shuttle bus
中尊寺.access_car: About 10 minutes from Tohoku Expressway 'Hiraizumi-Mizusawa IC'
中尊寺.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of a UNESCO World Heritage site adorned with vibrant autumn leaves.
中尊寺.desc:
Chuson-ji, founded by Master Jikaku, is a profound temple of the Tendai sect and a UNESCO World Heritage site. Every year, from early to mid-November, hundreds of Iroha maple and maple trees bloom around the solemn main hall and deep mountain area, transforming this sacred space into a stunning spectacle of color.

The most iconic view features the 'National Treasure Konjiki-do, ancient moss, and vibrant autumn leaves along the classical approach.' Colorful clusters of leaves spread along both sides of the long 'Tsukimi-zaka' ancient cedar-lined staircase, beautifully contrasting with the old stone lanterns. This harmonious scene perfectly embodies the tranquil historical Zen essence unique to ancient temples.

The path from the main gate to the main hall is a long, natural gravel slope. Visitors are strongly advised to wear slip-resistant shoes with good grip. During the autumn leaves festival, enchanting nighttime illuminations are held. Early mornings on weekdays offer the most ethereal atmosphere, ideal for capturing stunning cultural landscapes.

鳴子峽.count: massive autumn foliage in a 100-meter deep canyon
鳴子峽.address: 宮城縣大崎市鳴子溫泉星沼
鳴子峽.access_train: About a 30-minute walk from JR Nakayama-daira Onsen Station or take a temporary bus from Naruko Onsen Station
鳴子峽.access_car: Approximately 50 minutes from the Tohoku Expressway "Furukawa IC"
鳴子峽.desc:
Naruko Gorge is a majestic canyon, reaching depths of 100 meters, sculpted over the years by the Otakigawa River. Every year from late October to early November, thousands of maple and beech trees surrounding the steep white cliffs transform in unison, painting the entire mountainous gorge in a kaleidoscope of colors.

The most breathtaking highlight here is undoubtedly the legendary "Ooshibuzawa Bridge framed by autumn leaves." From the observation deck, the grand concrete arch bridge spans the colorful cliffs, and if you're lucky, you might catch a glimpse of the JR Rikuu East Line train emerging from a tunnel, weaving through the vibrant forest.

As one of Tohoku's top destinations, the Nakayama-daira Observation Deck is often bustling with photographers during the autumn foliage season. The park features a well-planned "Ooshibuzawa Walking Trail" with gentle elevation changes, so be sure to wear comfortable walking shoes. Visiting early on weekdays is the best way to avoid crowds and capture stunning views of the gorge.
鳴子峽.tagline: Experience the thunderous roar of the Otakigawa and be dazzled by a vibrant tapestry of colors.

秋保大瀑布.count: native forest surrounding the scenic waterfall
秋保大瀑布.address: 宮城縣仙台市太白區秋保町馬場大瀧
秋保大瀑布.access_train: Take a bus from JR Sendai Station to Akiu Great Falls, approximately 90 minutes.
秋保大瀑布.access_car: About 20 minutes from Sendai Minami IC on the Tohoku Expressway.
秋保大瀑布.tagline: Experience the thunderous beauty of Japan's top waterfall, surrounded by a vibrant tapestry of colorful blooms.
秋保大瀑布.desc:
Akiu Great Falls, nestled deep within the renowned Akiu Onsen area, boasts a stunning drop of 55 meters and is recognized as one of Japan's top waterfalls. From late October to early November, thousands of maple and beech trees burst into a riot of color around the majestic falls and the Natori River gorge.

The most breathtaking sight is the dramatic juxtaposition of the roaring waterfall and the expansive, colorful floral carpet. The towering falls cascade down with the force of galloping horses, while at your feet, a deep blue wall of flowers sprawls across the valley slope, creating a powerful visual experience that balances strength and grace.

The walking paths to the base of the falls consist mainly of natural dirt trails and steep rocky steps, with the abundant mist keeping the paths slippery year-round. It's essential to wear professional anti-slip hiking shoes. For a more leisurely view, the well-planned Takimidai trail at the summit offers the best lighting for photography on weekday mornings.

角館武家屋敷.count: About 400 maple trees along the historic street
角館武家屋敷.fee: 免費（部分設施參觀另計）
角館武家屋敷.address: 秋田縣仙北市角館町表町至東勝樂丁
角館武家屋敷.access_train: Approximately a 20-minute walk from JR Kakunodate Station
角館武家屋敷.access_car: About 40 minutes from the Akita Expressway, Omagari IC
角館武家屋敷.tagline: A samurai street painted in red, where Edo charm meets autumn hues.
角館武家屋敷.desc: Kakunodate is one of Japan's best-preserved samurai house streets. During the maple leaf season, the contrast of the black wooden walls and vibrant red leaves creates a dramatic autumn landscape. Stroll along the inner town, where the branches of century-old trees intertwine overhead, forming a golden-red canopy. This spot is a quintessential destination for autumn leaf viewing in Tohoku.

小安峽.count: native forests along both sides of Oosutobukigawa
小安峽.address: 秋田縣湯澤市皆瀨新處
小安峽.access_train: About 50 minutes by bus from JR Yuzawa Station
小安峽.access_car: About 40 minutes from Yuzawa IC on the Yuzawa Yokote Road
小安峽.tagline: A breathtaking canyon of hot springs, embraced by vibrant autumn leaves.
小安峽.desc: Koyasugawa is a spectacular canyon carved by the Gane River, where steaming hot springs flow year-round from the canyon walls, creating a unique geothermal landscape. During the autumn foliage season, take a walk along the cliffside trails, surrounded by cascading waterfalls and mountains ablaze with red leaves, and experience the stunning contrasts of fall.

山寺（立石寺）.access_train: About a 7-minute walk from JR Senseki Line "Yamadera Station" (to the trailhead)
山寺（立石寺）.access_car: Approximately 20 minutes from Yamagata Expressway "Yamagata Kita IC"
山寺（立石寺）.tagline: Step into a world of autumn hues and poetic inspiration.
山寺（立石寺）.desc: Yamadera (Risshaku-ji) is an ancient temple founded in 860 AD. As you ascend the 1,015 stone steps, you'll find a beautiful interplay of evergreens and maple trees. In autumn, deep reds and golden yellows unfold against the backdrop of stunning cliffs. The view of the valley below inspired Matsuo Bashō to pen the timeless verse, "The stillness seeps into the rock, the sound of cicadas," making this place even more enchanting in fall.

銀山溫泉.count: The native forest of Shirogane Park behind the onsen street
銀山溫泉.address: 山形縣尾花澤市大字銀山新畑
銀山溫泉.access_train: About a 40-minute bus ride from JR Oishida Station
銀山溫泉.access_car: General vehicles are prohibited from entering the onsen street; parking is available at designated lots with shuttle service.
銀山溫泉.tagline: A Taisho Romance: A Dreamy Autumn Night of Leaves and Lanterns
銀山溫泉.desc: Ginyama Onsen is renowned for its Taisho-era wooden ryokans lining the banks of the Ginyama River. As night falls, gas lamps illuminate the street, casting a warm glow on the babbling stream below. In autumn, the maple trees above the ryokans gradually turn vibrant shades of red, creating a stunning nightscape where the fiery leaves and the warm lights of the onsen street intertwine. This enchanting view is one of Japan's most talked-about autumn night scenes, making it feel like a journey through time.

磐梯吾妻 Skyline.hours: 24 小時開放（11月中旬起冬季封閉）
磐梯吾妻 Skyline.address: 福島縣福島市町庭坂至土湯溫泉町
磐梯吾妻 Skyline.access_train: Take a seasonal sightseeing bus from JR Fukushima Station.
磐梯吾妻 Skyline.access_car: About 30 minutes from Tohoku Expressway's Fukushima Nishi IC.
磐梯吾妻 Skyline.tagline: Breathtaking autumn views along a highland highway, framed by volcanic landscapes.
磐梯吾妻 Skyline.desc: 磐梯吾妻 Skyline is a scenic highland road stretching approximately 29 kilometers, passing through the volcanic terrain of Jōdobira and the area around Higashiyama Onsen. It’s the most magnificent autumn foliage route in Fukushima Prefecture. In early October, the shrubs and white birch trees along the route begin to change color, with vibrant yellows and oranges standing out against the stark volcanic landscape. On clear days, you can even catch a glimpse of the Pacific Ocean in the distance.

五色沼.types: maples, cherry blossoms, Japanese water oaks
五色沼.count: about 30 lakes surrounded by pristine forests
五色沼.address: 福島縣耶麻郡北鹽原村大字檜原
五色沼.access_train: Take a bus from JR Inawashiro Station for about 30 minutes
五色沼.access_car: Approximately 25 minutes from the Inawashiro Bandai Kogen IC on the Ban-etsu Expressway
五色沼.tagline: Emerald, indigo, and crimson—explore the mystical Five Color Lakes!
五色沼.desc: 五色沼 is a stunning cluster of lakes nestled within the lush forests of the Urabandai region. The unique minerals and light refraction create a mesmerizing palette of blues, greens, and emerald hues in the water. In autumn, vibrant red maple leaves surround the lakes, reflecting a dreamlike scenery on the water's surface. The 3.6-kilometer nature trail is the perfect way to easily connect the various ponds and soak in the beauty.

層雲峽.types: Iroha maple, large maple, Nanakamado maple
層雲峽.count: 24 kilometers of cliff walls, featuring the stunning "Meteor Waterfall" and "Galaxy Waterfall"
層雲峽.hours: 24 小時開放（黑岳纜車 06:00～18:00，視季節調整）
層雲峽.fee: 免費（纜車成人往返 2,400 円）
層雲峽.address: 北海道上川郡上川町層雲峽
層雲峽.access_train: About 35 minutes by bus from JR "Kamikawa Station"
層雲峽.access_car: About 25 minutes from "Asahikawa-Monbetsu Expressway" Kamikawa Sōunkyō IC
層雲峽.tagline: Gaze upon the cliffs of Daisetsuzan National Park, awed by the soaring conifers and fiery waterfalls beneath the Kurodake Ropeway.
層雲峽.desc:
Sōunkyō is located upstream of the Ishikari River and is renowned for its 24-kilometer stretch of impressive columnar jointed cliffs. Every year from late September to early October, the steep gray rock walls are adorned with thousands of flowering rowan and maple trees, transforming the deep valleys at the foot of Daisetsuzan into a vibrant tapestry of colors.

The most breathtaking highlight is undoubtedly the view of the "Meteor Galaxy Waterfall framed by colorful foliage." From the observation deck, two majestic waterfalls cascade down from the fiery cliffs, over 100 meters high. Additionally, taking the "Kurodake Ropeway" allows you to soar high above, offering a stunning aerial view of the colorful fields and coniferous forests below, resembling a three-dimensional carpet.

As the first valley in the northern region to welcome autumn leaves, temperatures in the mountains can drop close to freezing in early October. If you're planning to enjoy the fall colors and take photos, be sure to wear a thick coat that provides wind protection and warmth. The valley trails are well-planned and easy to navigate, and it's highly recommended to visit on a clear morning for the best visibility; the sunlight streaming into the valley enhances the richness of the autumn colors.

阿寒湖.count: The lakeside forest turns red, with the famous "Takiguchi" being a photographer's paradise
阿寒湖.fee: 免費（遊覽船大人 2,150 円）
阿寒湖.address: 北海道釧路市阿寒町阿寒湖溫泉
阿寒湖.access_train: About 2 hours by bus from JR "Kushiro Station"
阿寒湖.tagline: Discover the hidden volcanic paradise of Doto, where majestic Akankofu, ancient hot springs, and vibrant lakeside foliage await.
阿寒湖.desc:
Akankohan is a natural volcanic crater lake nestled within Akan Mashu National Park, renowned for its natural monument, green algae. From early to mid-October, the Japanese maple and beech trees lining the expansive lakeshore burst into a spectacular display of autumn colors, transforming this historic northern hot spring retreat into a vibrant canvas.

The most iconic scene is the "Takimi Bridge and the confluence of the Akankawa River," where visitors can capture the rushing clear stream splashing against the backdrop of golden and crimson maple forests from the rustic bridge. Additionally, a boat ride on the lake offers a perfect view of the azure waters and the towering Akankofu in the background.

The flat terrain and well-maintained trails around the lakeside hot spring area make it an ideal spot for a leisurely early autumn stroll with the whole family. Due to the strong winds and chilly temperatures in Doto during the mornings and evenings, be sure to bring warm, windproof clothing for your outdoor adventures. Visiting on a clear morning will provide the best chance to capture the tranquil lake surface and stunning reflections of the floral landscape.

五稜郭公園.count: The star-shaped fortress is adorned in shades of red and orange from the maple and cherry leaves.
五稜郭公園.fee: 免費（五稜郭塔大人 1,000 円）
五稜郭公園.address: 北海道函館市五稜郭町44
五稜郭公園.access_train: 15-minute walk from Hakodate Tram 'Goryokaku Koen Mae'
五稜郭公園.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of Hakodate's star-shaped fortress, blanketed in a vibrant tapestry of red and yellow leaves.
五稜郭公園.desc:
Goryokaku Park is a designated Special Historic Site featuring a unique star-shaped Western-style fortress. From mid-October to early November, the park's 1,600 cherry trees transform into a stunning display of red, adding rich autumn colors to this solemn battleground from the late Edo period.

The highlight is the panoramic view from the Goryokaku Tower. Standing at 107 meters, visitors can take in the breathtaking sight of the vast star-shaped flower carpet, surrounded by a moat, showcasing a striking contrast to the spring cherry blossoms.

The park's terrain is completely flat, and the pathways along the moat are fully accessible, making it very friendly for seniors and strollers. For the best chance to capture a clear star-shaped reflection, it's recommended to visit during calm mornings. In the afternoon to dusk, the interplay of light through the maple trees creates the perfect conditions for landscape photography.

大沼國定公園.count: The stunning reflections of Komagatake and 126 islands
大沼國定公園.address: 北海道龜田郡七飯町大沼町
大沼國定公園.access_train: About a 5-minute walk from JR Ōnuma Park Station
大沼國定公園.tagline: A tranquil oasis at the foot of Komagatake, capturing stunning views of islands and vibrant autumn foliage.
大沼國定公園.desc:
Ōnuma Quasi-National Park is listed among Japan's New Three Views, a vast waterside park surrounding Ōnuma, Kōnuma, and the majestic Komagatake volcano. Every year from mid to late October, the 126 islands scattered across the lake and the surrounding maple forests reach their peak, transforming the shoreline into a vibrant tapestry of colorful autumn leaves.

The park's core feature is the harmonious interplay of volcanic scenery, lakeside beauty, and wooden bridges connecting the islands. Bright red and yellow blooms line the gentle gravel paths, inviting travelers to stroll through classical arched bridges that connect the islands. When the calm water perfectly reflects the Komagatake volcano and the colorful foliage, the scene feels expansive and refreshing.

The park is incredibly spacious, and renting a bike at the park square is highly recommended for a leisurely exploration. The main flower viewing routes and waterside boardwalks are well-maintained, making for a comfortable walk. Early mornings offer the calmest lake surface, making it the best time to capture the fresh air and perfect reflections of the floral sea.

知床五湖.count: Reflections of autumn foliage from ancient forests and rolling peaks on the tranquil lake surface
知床五湖.fee: 免費（若走地面步道需付費參加講習，約 250 円）
知床五湖.address: 北海道斜里郡斜里町遠音別村
知床五湖.access_train: About 90 minutes by bus from JR Shiretoko Shari Station
知床五湖.tagline: Wander through a UNESCO World Heritage forest, awed by the vibrant autumn hues of Shiretoko's peaks and elevated boardwalks.
知床五湖.desc:
Shiretoko Goko, located on the Shiretoko Peninsula, is a UNESCO World Heritage site renowned for its pristine ecology and resident brown bears. Every year, from early to mid-October, the ancient forests surrounding five mysterious volcanic lakes burst into a tapestry of autumn colors, transforming this secluded valley into a vibrant early fall wonderland.

The most breathtaking sight is the "dreamy frame of the elevated boardwalk, the Shiretoko mountain range, and the lake's autumn foliage." Stroll along the 800-meter-long, barrier-free boardwalk, protected by an electric fence, where you can capture the stunning wall of colorful leaves in front of you, alongside the majestic Shiretoko peaks and the azure Sea of Okhotsk in the distance—it's simply breathtaking.

To access the other lakes beyond Lake One, you need to walk the "ground walking trail," which requires prior application and participation in a paid workshop. The weather in this highland area can change dramatically, with strong winds, so be sure to wear sturdy hiking shoes and carry warm, windproof clothing. Visiting on weekday mornings allows you to capture the ethereal morning mist enveloping the landscape.

十和田湖.count: Double crater lake surrounded by fiery red maple leaves and the "Statue of a Girl"
十和田湖.access_train: About 2 to 3 hours by bus from JR "Hachinohe Station" or "Aomori Station"
十和田湖.tagline: Discover the breathtaking beauty of Japan's dual volcanic lake, where legends and golden beech trees meet.
十和田湖.desc:
Towada Lake is a massive double volcanic lake straddling Aomori and Akita, recognized as a special scenic spot in Japan. From mid to late October, the expansive shores are adorned with vibrant Japanese beech and maple forests, transforming the deep blue waters into a spectacular display of color and grandeur.

The most iconic view can be found at the "Hakkoda Pass Observation Deck," where visitors can capture the majestic Okura Peninsula, the mirror-like lake, and the sprawling colorful forests below. Additionally, taking a ride on the "Towada Lake Sightseeing Boat" allows for an up-close experience of the stunning autumn foliage.

The lakeside walking trail is gently graded and perfect for a leisurely stroll along the water's edge in early autumn. Due to strong winds and chilly temperatures in the highland mornings and evenings, it's essential to bring warm, windproof clothing. For the best reflections of the vibrant foliage, visit on a clear, calm morning.

八甲田山.count: Famous for its 360-degree carpet of autumn leaves, with distant views of Mutsu Bay
八甲田山.fee: 免費（纜車大人往返 2,000 円）
八甲田山.address: 青森縣青森市荒川寒水澤
八甲田山.access_train: Take a bus from JR Aomori Station for about 1 hour to Hakkoda Ropeway Station
八甲田山.tagline: Experience the breathtaking autumn tapestry at the foot of the Hakkoda Ropeway!
八甲田山.desc:
Hakkoda Mountain is a majestic range formed by multiple active volcanoes, serving as the frontline for Japan's earliest autumn foliage on Honshu. From late September to mid-October, the alpine tundra plants at the summit begin to change color, followed by the maple and birch trees cascading down the slopes, painting the entire mountain in rich autumn hues.

The most stunning visual experience can be found aboard the Hakkoda Aerial Cableway. Travelers can ascend slowly and gaze down at the vast canvas of vibrant red and yellow foliage interspersed with lush green coniferous forests, creating a breathtaking natural palette that feels both expansive and striking.

Due to the rugged alpine terrain, temperatures at the summit can approach freezing in early October. Be sure to wear a professional mountaineering jacket that provides wind and warmth protection when coming to enjoy the autumn leaves. Weather in the mountains can change rapidly, so it's highly recommended to visit on a clear morning for the best visibility, as the sunlight enhances the vibrancy of the foliage.

城倉大橋.count: 360 meters long, overlooking 城倉溪谷 from a height of 122 meters
城倉大橋.address: 青森縣青森市荒川南部
城倉大橋.access_train: About 80 minutes by bus from JR 青森駅 to 城倉温泉, then a 20-minute walk
城倉大橋.tagline: Experience breathtaking views from Japan's longest steel arch bridge, surrounded by vibrant autumn colors.
城倉大橋.desc:
城倉大橋 spans across the 城倉溪谷, stretching 360 meters, making it Japan's longest steel arch bridge. Every year in mid-October, this magnificent modern structure transforms alongside the pristine forests at the foot of 大雪山, painting the deep valley in a stunning array of colors.

The visual highlight is the "epic pairing of the grand bridge and the steep valley's autumn foliage." Standing 100 meters above, you can gaze down at the cascading 清流 of 城倉川, flanked by cliffs adorned with golden beech and fiery maple trees, offering a breathtaking 360-degree panoramic view.

Both ends of the bridge feature well-maintained observation parking areas and flat walkways, making it easy to stroll and enjoy the autumn leaves. Due to the strong winds at this high altitude, be sure to secure your hat and tripod while taking photos. Aim to visit around noon for optimal sunlight, which enhances the vibrancy of the autumn colors.

中野紅葉山.count: Known as 'Koarashiyama', with waterfalls and red torii gates adorning the maple forest
中野紅葉山.address: 青森縣黑石市中野不動舘27
中野紅葉山.access_train: About 30 minutes by bus from Kōnan Railway 'Kuroishi Station'
中野紅葉山.tagline: Explore the sacred realm of Nakano, where vibrant autumn leaves and ancient shrines await.
中野紅葉山.desc:
Nakano Momijiyama is a historic spot for viewing autumn leaves, believed to have been introduced by the lord of Hirosaki from Kyoto. From mid-October to early November, the area surrounding Nakano Shrine and Nakano River Valley bursts into color with hundreds of ancient maple trees and groves, transforming this sacred domain into a vibrant tapestry of hues.

The most iconic scene here features a dialogue between the vermilion torii gate, Fudo Waterfall, and a cluster of blooming flowers. Colorful floral balls embrace the rustic wooden shrine, moss-covered stone steps, and flowing streams. When the lights illuminate the area at night, the entire mountain transforms into a golden world, exuding a mystical and fantastical atmosphere.

The trails are built among the natural hilly valleys, with some sections featuring gravel steps and slopes. It is highly recommended to wear non-slip casual shoes when visiting for flower viewing. Early morning visits on weekdays offer a serene experience, allowing you to enjoy the ethereal Zen-like ambiance of the mountains, with mist swirling around the ancient scenery, perfect for photography.

毛越寺.count: UNESCO World Heritage site, autumn leaves reflecting in the Great Fountain Pond
毛越寺.address: 岩手縣西磐井郡平泉町平泉大澤58
毛越寺.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from JR "Hiraizumi Station"
毛越寺.tagline: Stroll through a serene paradise, captivated by the vibrant blooms along the shores of the Great Fountain Pond.
毛越寺.desc:
毛越寺 is a UNESCO World Heritage site renowned for its beautifully preserved "Pure Land Garden" established by the revered monk Jikaku Daishi. From late October to mid-November, the maple trees surrounding the expansive Great Fountain Pond reach their peak, transforming the entire classical Japanese garden into a colorful spectacle.

The visual highlight is the "exquisite classical pond, unique rocky islets, and the mirror-like interplay of autumn leaves." Vibrant flower clusters bloom alongside the gently winding gravel paths, and when the calm, mirror-like surface of the water perfectly reflects the blue and purple flowers and the distant temple ruins, the scene feels expansive, radiating an elegant Eastern Zen essence.

The garden's flat terrain and well-maintained walking paths make it very accessible for strollers and seniors with mobility issues. It's recommended to set aside an hour for a leisurely stroll, especially in the early morning when crowds are minimal, and sunlight filters through the treetops, casting beautiful reflections on the flower walls.

嚴美溪.count: Dramatic rock formations and rushing waters paired with vibrant autumn leaves create a stunning contrast.
嚴美溪.address: 岩手縣一關市嚴美町瀧之上
嚴美溪.access_train: About 20 minutes by bus from JR Ichinoseki Station
嚴美溪.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of vibrant autumn colors and majestic rock formations at the foot of Kurikoma Mountain.
嚴美溪.desc:
Yanbikyo is a stunning gorge carved by the Iwai River over many years, renowned for its steep cliffs and unique pothole formations. From mid-October to early November, the maple and beech trees lining the riverbanks transform into a vibrant tapestry of colors, bringing this natural wonder to life.

The most striking visual experience is the "clear stream, potholes, and fiery maple reflections in conversation." Standing on the Tenkou Bridge, you can see the rushing clear waters creating white splashes as they weave through the golden canopy of trees. Additionally, the famous local delicacy "Kogakudango," delivered across the river by a ropeway, offers a classic cultural experience while enjoying the autumn foliage.

The gorge's walking trails are flat and well-maintained, making it perfect for a leisurely family hike along the riverside. Since many of the main viewpoints are located on open rocky plateaus, it is highly recommended to bring sun protection and visit in the morning for the most comfortable experience and ideal photography opportunities.

猊鼻溪.count: Ride a wooden boat through 100-meter-high cliffs adorned with autumn leaves
猊鼻溪.fee: 免費（遊船大人往返 1,800 円）
猊鼻溪.address: 岩手縣一關市東山町長坂字町467
猊鼻溪.access_train: About a 5-minute walk from JR Nihonbashi Station
猊鼻溪.tagline: Glide through breathtaking cliffs and vibrant autumn colors on a traditional wooden boat.
猊鼻溪.desc:
猊鼻溪 is a stunning gorge carved by the Sandai River, recognized as one of Japan's top scenic spots. From mid-October to early November, the towering gray limestone cliffs, reaching up to 100 meters, transform alongside the maple trees at the summit, painting the deep valley in a spectacular array of colors.

The most captivating experience here is aboard the "Nihonbashi Boat Song Traditional Rowboat." Visitors can leisurely glide into the mountains on a vintage wooden flat-bottom boat, listening to the melodious folk songs of the boatman. On the right, the serene emerald stream flows gently, while on the left, a vibrant tapestry of colorful flowers cascades down the steep cliffs.

As the entire area is a natural deep valley, the riverside can be breezy and slightly cool, so be sure to bring a light jacket for your autumn leaf viewing. The round trip on the boat takes about 90 minutes, with a completely flat terrain, making it perfect for family outings. Opt for a morning departure when the sunlight best illuminates the V-shaped valley floor.

盛岡城跡公園.count: Ancient stone walls intertwined with fiery red maple leaves
盛岡城跡公園.access_train: About 10 minutes by loop bus from JR Morioka Station
盛岡城跡公園.tagline: Experience the vibrant autumn colors at the historic site of Morioka Castle!
盛岡城跡公園.desc:
Morioka Castle Ruins Park is built on the site of the former Morioka Castle, a stronghold of the Nanbu clan during the Edo period, and is recognized as one of Japan's top 100 castles. From late October to early November, the park's famous maple trees reach their peak, with hundreds of vibrant Iroha maple trees bursting into color, beautifully complementing the majestic stone walls of the ruins in early autumn hues.

The visual highlight here is the striking contrast between the unique granite stone walls and the blooming flowers. Strolling along the well-maintained paths, you can see bright red and deep purple flower clusters harmonizing with the ancient, sturdy white stone ramparts, showcasing a blend of history and nature.

The park is built on a hilly terrain, so some paths have inclines. It's highly recommended to wear non-slip athletic shoes when visiting for flower viewing. The park features spacious areas and large grassy squares, making it a comfortable spot for leisurely walks. Early mornings on weekdays are the least crowded, perfect for a relaxing stroll and capturing stunning photos.

松島（圓通院）.count: The illuminated reflections of maple leaves in the "Shinzi Pond" are stunningly beautiful.
松島（圓通院）.fee: 大人 500 円（夜間點燈另計）
松島（圓通院）.address: 宮城縣宮城郡松島町松島町內67
松島（圓通院）.access_train: About a 5-minute walk from JR Senseki Line "Matsushima Kaigan Station"
松島（圓通院）.tagline: Explore the historic Enzoin Temple, where the heart-shaped pond reflects vibrant autumn leaves.
松島（圓通院）.desc:
Enzoin, located in Matsushima, one of Japan's top three scenic views, is an ancient temple of the Rinzai sect. From late October to late November, the expansive grounds, shaped by the mountains, come alive with hundreds of maple trees and maple groves changing colors, adorning this sacred space in a vibrant, lively display.

The temple is famous for its limited-time "Maple Night Illumination". Standing by the tranquil "Shinzi Pond," the brilliant red maple trees and the surrounding Japanese rock garden perfectly reflect on the water's surface. When the gentle breeze blows, the scene becomes rich in layers, revealing the unique silent history and Zen essence of this ancient temple.

The temple grounds are flat and compact, making for an easy stroll, with a total visit time of about forty minutes. The lines for the night illumination can be quite long, so it's best to visit early on a weekday right when the doors open to capture the pristine sacred space and perfect reflections.

定禪寺通.count: The massive ginkgo trees turn a vibrant golden orange, showcasing the city's autumn colors.
定禪寺通.address: 宮城縣仙台市青葉區國分町
定禪寺通.access_train: Just get off at the subway station "Koutoudai Park Station" to reach it.
定禪寺通.tagline: Stroll through Sendai's green heart, dazzled by a golden tunnel of towering ginkgo trees.
定禪寺通.desc:
定禪寺通 is a signature tree-lined avenue that runs through the heart of Sendai's bustling commercial district, recognized as one of Japan's top 100 roads. From late October to early November, the 166 towering ginkgo trees lining both sides of the central walkway reach their peak, transforming into a brilliant display of golden hues.

The main attraction is the "golden tunnel" created by the long avenue. As you leisurely stroll along the smooth central path, the lush golden branches intertwine above, framing the Western sculpted bronze statues nestled among the trees, showcasing Sendai's unique blend of refined modern urban aesthetics.

Located in the city center, this spot boasts excellent transportation and amenities, making it perfect for travelers looking for a carefree morning walk. The avenue is fully flat and well-maintained, making it very stroller and wheelchair friendly. Arriving early on weekdays before dawn allows you to enjoy pristine street scenes and exceptional urban lighting for photography.

藏王 Eco-line.count: Significant elevation changes reveal distinct layers of autumn scenery.
藏王 Eco-line.hours: 24 小時（11月上旬至4月下旬冬季封閉）
藏王 Eco-line.access_car: About 1 hour from Shiroishi IC or Murata IC on the Tohoku Expressway.
藏王 Eco-line.tagline: Experience the vibrant colors of nature along the breathtaking Zao Eco-Line.
藏王 Eco-line.desc:
Zao Eco-Line (蔵王エコーライン) is a scenic drive that stretches across Miyagi and Yamagata, soaring over a thousand meters above sea level on a volcanic plateau. From late September to mid-October each year, thousands of maple and beech trees transform into a stunning tunnel of colors, creating a truly picturesque route.

The highlight of this journey is the "three-dimensional colorful flower belt" that winds along the mountain road. Whether you roll down your windows for a leisurely drive or stop by the roadside, you'll be surrounded by vibrant clusters of red and yellow blooms. The colorful flower belt cascades with the terrain, beautifully complemented by the mysterious crater lake, Okama, in the distance.

As this attraction is primarily an open road for vehicles, it will close for winter due to snowfall in late October, so be sure to check the latest official announcements before you go. The mountain peaks can be quite windy and cool, so don’t forget to bring a warm jacket, and driving on a clear morning will provide the most comfortable experience.

抱返溪谷.count: The striking contrast between 'Hōhaisen Blue' waters and vibrant red leaves
抱返溪谷.address: 秋田縣仙北市田澤湖卒田
抱返溪谷.access_train: About 15 minutes by temporary bus or taxi from JR Kakunodate Station
抱返溪谷.tagline: A hidden gem where vibrant autumn leaves reflect in crystal-clear waters.
抱返溪谷.desc: Hōhaisen Keikoku, named for its narrow mountain path where travelers must hug the rocks to pass, features a stunning blue stream flowing through the canyon. The banks are adorned with layers of fiery red leaves, creating a breathtaking scene mirrored in the water. The trail leading to the 'Waterfall of Reflection' offers picturesque views, making it one of Akita Prefecture's most beloved secret spots for autumn foliage viewing.

田澤湖.count: The golden statue of Tatsuko and the deep blue lake water create a stunning contrast with the autumn leaves.
田澤湖.access_train: About a 15-minute bus ride from JR Tazawako Station
田澤湖.tagline: Japan's deepest lake, where autumn colors dance on emerald blue.
田澤湖.desc: Tazawako is Japan's deepest lake, its waters shimmering in a unique cobalt hue. In autumn, the surrounding hills transform with vibrant red leaves, golden yellows, and fiery oranges reflecting on the lake's surface, beautifully framing the statue of Tatsuko-hime. The lake's circumference is about 20 kilometers, making cycling around it the perfect way to take in the stunning autumn scenery.

秋田駒岳.count: From above, the stunning red leaves blanket the hills and valleys surrounding Lake Tazawa.
秋田駒岳.address: 秋田縣仙北市田澤湖生保內
秋田駒岳.access_train: Take a bus from JR Tazawako Station to Komagatake Hachigome.
秋田駒岳.tagline: Experience the first hues of autumn in Tohoku's majestic mountains.
秋田駒岳.desc: Akita Komagatake is one of the first mountains in the Tohoku region to welcome the vibrant colors of autumn. Every year, from mid-September, the alpine meadows above 1,600 meters begin to transform into shades of orange and gold. After taking the cable car to the summit, the grassy areas around Amida Pond and the distant view of Iwate Mountain create a breathtaking autumn panorama, making it a top choice for mountain photography enthusiasts.

藏王連峰.count: A unique landscape of emerald crater lake and changing alpine flora
藏王連峰.access_train: About 90 minutes by bus from JR Yamagata Station
藏王連峰.tagline: Majestic autumn colors by the crater lake await you!
藏王連峰.desc: The Zao Mountain Range spans Yamagata and Miyagi Prefectures, with autumn foliage beginning to spread around the crater lake, Okama, at the summit. The vibrant green lake contrasts beautifully with the gradually reddening grass slopes, creating a spectacular scene. The Zao Echo Line is fully accessible by car, offering rich layers of autumn scenery from various elevations, making it the most iconic maple leaf route in Yamagata.

最上川.count: Sit comfortably on a heated boat and enjoy the view of the maple forests along the Mogami Gorge.
最上川.address: 山形縣最上郡戶澤村古口
最上川.access_train: About an 8-minute walk from JR Rikuu West Line "Furukuchi Station" to the boarding area.
最上川.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of autumn leaves along Japan's majestic rivers.
最上川.desc:
Mogami River is one of Japan's three great rapid rivers. Glide along in a traditional Mogami River boat, serenaded by the boatman's folk songs, as you slowly traverse the banks adorned with vibrant autumn foliage. Matsuo Basho even captured the essence of this journey in his work, "Oku no Hosomichi," where the falling leaves drift on the water's surface, creating a flowing ink painting of nature's beauty.

The best time to visit is from late October to early November, when the maple and beech trees are in full display. Sit comfortably on a heated boat and take in the stunning views of the maple forests lining both sides of the Mogami Gorge.

月山.count: Famous for its vast golden-red grasslands and wide vistas
月山.address: 山形縣東田川郡庄內町
月山.access_train: Take a bus from JR Tsuruoka Station to the eighth station
月山.tagline: Experience breathtaking autumn colors at the sacred peak of Dewa Sanzan!
月山.desc: Mt. Gassan is the highest peak of Dewa Sanzan and a premier spot for autumn foliage in Yamagata Prefecture. Starting in mid-September, the grasslands at the eighth station begin to turn vibrant red. Take the aerial cable car to the summit for stunning panoramic views of the expansive autumn landscape. Near the top, Gassan Shrine reflects the brilliant fall colors, creating a majestic and solemn atmosphere.

霞城公園.count: The maple leaves and historic sites around the moat and castle gate create a perfect harmony.
霞城公園.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from JR Yamagata Station
霞城公園.tagline: Autumn splendor at Yamagata Castle ruins, where the moat reflects golden hues.
霞城公園.desc: Kajo Park is a city park centered around the ruins of the old Yamagata Castle's Ninomaru. The banks of the moat are lined with numerous maple and ginkgo trees, creating a stunning tapestry of gold and red in the fall. The stone walls and the restored Ichimonji gate, framed by fallen leaves, evoke the serene atmosphere of a historic samurai capital, making it a beloved spot for locals to enjoy autumn foliage walks in Yamagata City.

大內宿.count: The red leaves against the thatched roofs and surrounding mountains feel like a journey back in time.
大內宿.address: 福島縣南會津郡下鄉町大內
大內宿.access_train: About 20 minutes by bus from Yunokami Onsen Station on the Saruyuu Line.
大內宿.tagline: Thatched-roof streetscapes, a nostalgic autumn in an Edo-era post town.
大內宿.desc: Ōuchi-juku is a significant preservation area of traditional buildings that retain the charm of an Edo-period post town. Two rows of thatched-roof houses stretch along the stone-paved road, surrounded by hills blanketed in vibrant autumn leaves. In fall, deep red maple leaves adorn the rustic thatched roofs, creating a scene reminiscent of a still-life period drama. It's one of Japan's most nostalgic spots for enjoying autumn foliage.

鶴之城.count: The red tiles harmonize beautifully with the surrounding fiery maple leaves.
鶴之城.fee: 免費（天守閣入館 410 円）
鶴之城.address: 福島縣會津若松市追手町1-1
鶴之城.access_train: Take a circular bus from JR 'Aizuwakamatsu Station' to 'Tsuruno-jō Entrance'.
鶴之城.tagline: Red-roofed castle meets fiery autumn leaves, the last glory of Aizu.
鶴之城.desc: Tsuruno-jō (Wakamatsu Castle) is a historic symbol of the Aizu domain, renowned for its unique red-tiled roofs. In autumn, the maple leaves along the moat and around the castle gradually turn vibrant shades of red, beautifully contrasting with the striking red tiles, creating a rich and solemn tapestry of colors. The nighttime illumination of the maple leaves transforms the entire castle into a floating spectacle amidst a sea of red, making it one of the most iconic autumn sights in Aizu.

塔之岪.count: The combination of towering rock formations, suspension bridges, and autumn leaves creates a striking scene.
塔之岪.address: 福島縣南會津郡下鄉町彌五島
塔之岪.access_train: Aizu Railway, about a 3-minute walk from 塔之岪 Station.
塔之岪.tagline: Autumn's fiery embrace in a hidden canyon of giants.
塔之岪.desc: 塔之岪 is a stunning canyon along the Shidikawa River, where towering rock pillars reach for the sky, surrounded by vibrant maple leaves that turn a brilliant red in autumn. Although access is a bit challenging, this seclusion has preserved its pristine natural beauty. From the observation deck, gaze down at the cascading waterfalls intertwined with the fiery hues of the mountains, making it one of Fukushima's most breathtaking hidden gems for fall foliage.

筑波山.access_train: Take a bus from Tsukuba Express "Tsukuba Station" for about 40 minutes to "Tsukuba-san Shrine Entrance."
筑波山.access_car: About a 40-minute drive from the Joban Expressway "Tsuchiura Kita IC."
筑波山.tagline: A majestic twin-peaked mountain overlooking a tapestry of autumn colors in the Kanto Plain.
筑波山.desc: Tsukuba-san is a renowned sacred mountain in the Kanto Plain, celebrated for its graceful shape and historically known as "Nishi Fuji, Higashi Tsukuba." In autumn, the maple trees and mixed forests on the mountainside transform into a vibrant palette of colors. Riding the cable car or aerial tramway to the summit offers breathtaking views of the crimson leaves sprawling across the vast Kanto Plain. The maple trees around Tsukuba-san Shrine are particularly dense, making it one of Ibaraki Prefecture's most popular spots for autumn foliage.

花貫溪谷.count: A continuous stretch of autumn leaves along the valley
花貫溪谷.access_train: About a 20-minute taxi ride from JR Joban Line 'Takahagi Station'
花貫溪谷.access_car: Approximately a 15-minute drive from Joban Expressway 'Takahagi IC'
花貫溪谷.tagline: A breathtaking tunnel of autumn leaves on a suspension bridge, Ibaraki's late-fall masterpiece.
花貫溪谷.desc: Hanakuni Keikoku is Ibaraki Prefecture's ultimate spot for late and vibrant autumn foliage. The maple trees lining the stream create a natural tunnel of red leaves, with the Shiohime Suspension Bridge being the most famous photo spot. During the peak leaf season, limited traffic regulations are in place, requiring visitors to walk in, which helps preserve the tranquil atmosphere for leaf peeping. It's the perfect escape from the crowds, allowing you to deeply experience the beauty of autumn.

龍神大吊橋.count: Surrounding native forest in the canyon
龍神大吊橋.address: 茨城縣常陸太田市天下野町2133-6
龍神大吊橋.access_train: About 40 minutes by taxi from JR Suigun Line 'Hitachi-Ota Station'
龍神大吊橋.access_car: Approximately 1 hour from Nakak IC on the Joban Expressway
龍神大吊橋.tagline: Japan's longest pedestrian suspension bridge, overlooking the autumn colors of Lake Ryujin.
龍神大吊橋.desc: Ryujin Oozukyo Bridge is the longest pedestrian suspension bridge in Honshu, stretching 375 meters across the Ryujin Lake reservoir gorge, with a height of 100 meters above the water surface. During the maple leaf season, the vibrant reds of the leaves burst forth from both ends of the bridge and the canyon walls. Standing on the bridge and looking down, you'll be mesmerized by the stunning view where the fiery foliage meets the emerald waters. This season also offers thrilling bungee jumping activities for the adventurous!

龍王峽.count: The gorge is approximately 3 kilometers long.
龍王峽.address: 栃木縣日光市藤原龍王峽
龍王峽.access_train: A short walk from Ryūōkyō Station on the Yagan Railway.
龍王峽.access_car: About a 30-minute drive from the Imaichi IC on the Nikko Utsunomiya Road.
龍王峽.tagline: Experience the roar of nature and vibrant autumn colors at Ryūōkyō!
龍王峽.desc:
Ryūōkyō is a stunning gorge formed over 20 million years ago by volcanic activity from the Kinugawa River, named for its dragon-like shape. From late October to early November, the towering maple and beech trees on the steep cliffs of the white and blue dragons burst into a riot of colors, painting the entire valley in vivid hues.

The visual highlight is the "dialogue of strength and grace" between the Nijimi Bridge, Nijimi Falls, and the three-dimensional colorful forest. Strolling along the walking path, you can see the mist from the waterfall refracting beautiful rainbows in the sunlight, complemented by the scattered red and yellow maple trees and the rustic Gokōjin Shrine on the cliffs, creating a breathtaking scene.

The main maple viewing route is built within the natural creek valley, with some sections featuring natural stone steps and wooden walkways. It is highly recommended to wear non-slip casual shoes when visiting. The sunlight in this deep mountain area is limited, so it's best to come between 10 AM and noon to capture the most vibrant colors of the valley landscape.

那須高原.count: The entire highland is adorned with autumn leaves
那須高原.address: 栃木縣那須郡那須町湯本
那須高原.access_train: Take a bus from JR Kuroiso Station for about 40 minutes to Nasu Yumoto
那須高原.access_car: Approximately a 20-minute drive from the Tohoku Expressway Nasu IC
那須高原.tagline: Ascend the Nasu Highlands and behold the fiery red tapestry of autumn.
那須高原.desc:
Nasu Kogen, located in the northern part of Tochigi Prefecture, centers around the majestic active volcano, Chausudake, and is one of the first places in the Kanto region to showcase stunning autumn foliage. From late September to mid-October, the vibrant colors begin at the summit of Ubanohira and cascade down the mountainside, painting the highlands in rich hues of fiery rowan and golden birch.

The most breathtaking visual highlight is the ride on the Nasu Ropeway. Travelers can gently ascend and gaze down at the sprawling, colorful forest of yellow and red, intermingled with the rugged volcanic cliffs, creating a vast natural palette. The expansive scenery reveals the ultimate beauty of the wilderness.

Due to its high-altitude terrain, temperatures at the summit can drop close to single digits by early October. If you're planning to enjoy the autumn leaves and take photos, be sure to wear a professional windproof and warm jacket along with comfortable hiking shoes. The mountain weather can change rapidly, so it's highly recommended to visit on a clear morning for the best visibility and lighting effects.

鹽原溫泉.address: 栃木縣那須塩原市塩原
鹽原溫泉.access_train: Take a bus from JR Nishinasuno Station for about 40 minutes to Yunabara Onsen Bus Terminal.
鹽原溫泉.access_car: About a 20-minute drive from the Tōhoku Expressway Nishinasuno-Yahikogen IC.
鹽原溫泉.tagline: Stroll the breathtaking Sōbaku River's grand suspension bridges and immerse yourself in a summer symphony of vibrant red leaves and stunning waterfalls.
鹽原溫泉.desc:
Yunabara Onsen is nestled in the Sōbaku River gorge, a premier hot spring destination connected by multiple giant suspension bridges. From late October to mid-November, the pristine forests flanking the hot spring street reach their peak, blanketing the lush green gorge in a riot of colorful autumn leaves.

The most iconic view is undoubtedly the perfect frame of the "Red Suspension Bridge and Myōun-ji Temple's autumn foliage." This wooden bridge spans the clear stream, with a breathtaking carpet of brilliant red maple trees beneath your feet, harmonizing beautifully with the moss-covered ancient temple and historic stone markers. As a gentle breeze sweeps across the water's surface, it evokes a refreshing and ethereal Japanese landscape.

The riverside promenade in the hot spring area is well-maintained and perfect for a leisurely morning stroll or a day of soaking in the hot springs. Due to the limited width of the main viewing bridge, please avoid occupying the passage for extended periods during photo sessions. It is highly recommended to visit in the early morning, when the mist shrouds the gorge, creating the most ethereal scenery.

榛名山/榛名湖.count: widespread autumn leaves along the lakeside and foothills
榛名山/榛名湖.address: 群馬縣高崎市榛名湖町
榛名山/榛名湖.access_train: Take a bus from JR Takasaki Station for about 90 minutes to Haruna Lake
榛名山/榛名湖.access_car: About a 50-minute drive from Maebashi IC on the Kanetsu Expressway
榛名山/榛名湖.tagline: Experience the legendary volcanic beauty of Haruna and its stunning autumn colors.
榛名山/榛名湖.desc:
Haruna is a majestic mountain range formed by several massive active volcanoes, with the central crater lake, Haruna Lake, serving as a high-altitude summer oasis. From late October to early November, the gentle beech and maple forests surrounding the tranquil lakeside reach their peak, painting the entire mountain in vibrant colors.

The visual highlight is the stunning interplay of Haruna Fuji, the lakeshore, and the autumn leaves. Strips of red and yellow foliage stretch alongside the calm waters, creating a breathtaking scene where the clear surface perfectly reflects the conical shape of the mountain and the lush, colorful forest. This dynamic beauty offers an expansive sense of space.

The lakeside road is well-planned, making it ideal for leisurely drives or carefree bike rides. You can also take the Haruna Mountain Ropeway directly to the summit. Due to strong morning winds at the high-altitude lake, be sure to bring a warm jacket for your photo shoots. Visiting on a clear, windless morning will give you the best chance to capture stunning mirror-like views.

谷川岳.count: extensive autumn foliage from the foothills to mid-mountain
谷川岳.address: 群馬縣利根郡みなかみ町湯檜曾字天神平
谷川岳.access_train: Take the JR Joetsu Shinkansen to Jomo-Kogen Station, then transfer to a bus for about 30 minutes to "Tanigawadake Ropeway."
谷川岳.access_car: About a 15-minute drive from the Kanetsu Expressway "Minakami IC."
谷川岳.tagline: Experience the breathtaking tapestry of colors beneath the majestic Tanigawadake.
谷川岳.desc:
Tanigawadake is one of Japan's top 100 mountains, renowned for its dramatic cliffs and the towering rock faces of Ichinokura-sawa. Every year, from early to late October, the vibrant autumn leaves begin to cascade down from the alpine tundra at the summit, transforming the deep valley into a stunning display of reds and purples, making it a premier destination for fall foliage in the Kanto region.

The most exhilarating experience here is riding the "Tanigawadake Aerial Cableway." Visitors can ascend slowly, taking in breathtaking views of the colorful forest below, where the vibrant yellows and reds intermingle with the steep, rugged rocks, creating a vast natural palette that is visually striking and expansive.

The walking path to Ichinokura-sawa is relatively flat, but due to the unpredictable weather in high-altitude areas, temperatures in mid-October can drop to single digits. Be sure to wear professional running shoes and a warm, windproof jacket for your autumn leaf-peeping adventure. It's highly recommended to hike on a clear morning to capture the most magnificent views with the best visibility.

尾瀨.count: vast primary forests surrounding the highland wetland
尾瀨.hours: 依登山道行程而定（尾瀨ヶ原入口 05:00～）
尾瀨.fee: 免費（部分入山設施收費）
尾瀨.address: 群馬縣利根郡片品村戸倉
尾瀨.access_train: Take a bus from JR Numata Station or Jōmō-Kōgen Station to Oze Tokura, then transfer.
尾瀨.access_car: About a 40-minute drive from Kan-Etsu Expressway Numata IC to Oze parking lot.
尾瀨.tagline: Explore Japan's largest alpine wetland, where golden grasses and autumn leaves dance under the majestic backdrop of Suitsuzan.
尾瀨.desc:
Oze National Park is Japan's largest alpine wetland, spanning multiple prefectures and reaching an elevation of 1,400 meters. Due to its high altitude and latitude, the autumn leaves here begin to change earlier than anywhere else in Japan, from mid-September to early October. The expansive wooden walkways are flanked by herbaceous plants that transform into a brilliant sea of gold.

The park's striking visual feature is the harmonious blend of golden grasses, highland ponds, and the towering Suitsuzan. Stroll along the well-maintained, gentle wooden paths, surrounded by fields of golden grass nestled among clear, peat-filled pools. When the bright blue sky reflects on the water's surface, it creates an ethereal beauty that captivates the senses.

To protect the delicate natural ecosystem, private vehicles are completely prohibited year-round. Visitors must take an eco-friendly bus to the various trailheads. While the wetland paths are flat and easy to navigate, the journey is long and temperatures can be low, so be sure to wear professional hiking shoes and bring windproof and warm clothing. Early morning visits on weekdays offer a serene experience.

赤城山.count: extensive autumn foliage around Onuma Lake
赤城山.address: 群馬縣前橋市富士見町赤城山
赤城山.access_train: Take a bus from JR "Maebashi Station" for about 80 minutes to "Odo"
