赤城山.access_car: Approximately 40 minutes from Kanetsu Expressway "Maebashi IC"
赤城山.tagline: Embrace the serene beauty of Akagi Mountain's vibrant autumn hues.
赤城山.desc:
Akagi Mountain is a historic stratovolcano, renowned as one of the Three Sacred Mountains of Jomo alongside Haruna and Myogi. Every year, from early to late October, the area around the volcanic lake "Onuma" at the mountain's summit transforms into a stunning display of autumn foliage. Towering maple and beech trees turn into a colorful forest, adding a touch of early autumn beauty to this sacred domain.

The most iconic scene features the "Akagi Shrine's Woodpecker Bridge" framed by the lake's autumn leaves. The vibrant colors of the forest embrace the ancient wooden shrine, while the vermilion bridge stretches over the tranquil waters, creating a harmonious blend of movement and stillness. Behind it lies the endless blue of the lake, adorned with a tapestry of colorful foliage, showcasing the essence of Japanese aesthetic beauty.

The trails around Onuma are well-planned and easy to navigate, making it perfect for a leisurely lakeside hike. Due to the strong winds in the mornings and evenings at this high-altitude lake, it can feel quite cool, so be sure to bring a light jacket for warmth. Visiting on a clear, windless morning will give you the best chance to capture the ethereal reflections that fill the air with a sense of Zen.

草津溫泉.count: around the hot spring town and the approach to Kousen-ji Temple
草津溫泉.address: 群馬縣吾妻郡草津町草津
草津溫泉.access_train: Take a bus from JR Nagano-Hara Kusatsu-guchi Station for about 25 minutes to Kusatsu Onsen Bus Terminal.
草津溫泉.access_car: Approximately 90 minutes from Shibukawa Ikaho IC on the Kan-Etsu Expressway.
草津溫泉.tagline: Discover Japan's famed hot springs and vibrant autumn foliage at Kusatsu Onsen.
草津溫泉.desc:
Kusatsu Onsen is renowned worldwide for its impressive hot spring output and traditional "yuyuu" culture. From mid-October to early November, the nearby Nishi-no-Kawara Park comes alive with stunning autumn colors, as the mountains are adorned with vibrant maple and larch trees.

The visual highlight is the "fiery geothermal streams, rugged cliffs, and the dialogue of autumn leaves." The quaint stone paths and emerald hot spring pools are enveloped by a tapestry of pink and red foliage. As the steaming white mist weaves through the golden canopies, the scene takes on an enchanting, mystical quality.

The park's gentle terrain and well-maintained walking paths make for a comfortable stroll. Being a world-class attraction, it draws large crowds during the day. It is highly recommended to visit early on weekdays or during the evening when the lights come on, allowing you to capture the essence of this cultural landscape through the steam and colorful trees.

伊香保溫泉（河鹿橋）.address: 群馬縣渋川市伊香保町伊香保
伊香保溫泉（河鹿橋）.access_train: Take a bus from JR "Shibukawa Station" for about 20 minutes to "Ikaho Onsen"
伊香保溫泉（河鹿橋）.access_car: About a 20-minute drive from Kanetsu Expressway "Shibukawa Ikaho IC"
伊香保溫泉（河鹿橋）.tagline: Discover the vibrant red bridge nestled in the depths of Ishidan Street, illuminated by the enchanting Iroha maple lights.
伊香保溫泉（河鹿橋）.desc:
Ikaho Onsen is an ancient hot spring town famous for its 365 stone steps. From late October to mid-November, the "Kashikobashi" bridge at the top of Ishidan Street becomes a breathtaking sight as hundreds of Iroha maples and surrounding trees turn brilliant shades of red, adding a splash of early autumn color to this historic hot spring town.

The most stunning highlight is undoubtedly the "crimson Kashikobashi bridge framed by a sea of autumn leaves." This vibrant arched bridge spans the hot spring stream, with a cascade of red and yellow maple leaves at your feet. When the night lights are turned on, the entire mountain transforms into a golden world, showcasing a captivating blend of Eastern religious aesthetics and fantasy style.

To reach Kashikobashi, you'll need to climb some of the stone steps along Ishidan Street, so it's highly recommended to wear non-slip athletic shoes for leaf viewing. During the night illumination period, crowds can be quite large, so to avoid the throngs and capture the perfect shot of the bridge surrounded by autumn leaves, it's best to arrive early on a weekday before dawn.

長瀞溪谷.count: extensive autumn foliage along the valley
長瀞溪谷.fee: 免費（遊覽船大人 1,600 円）
長瀞溪谷.address: 埼玉縣秩父郡長瀞町長瀞
長瀞溪谷.access_train: A short walk from Chichibu Railway "Nagatoro Station"
長瀞溪谷.access_car: About a 30-minute drive from Hanazono IC on the Kanetsu Expressway.
長瀞溪谷.tagline: Sail the Arakawa in a traditional wooden boat and be dazzled by a vibrant tapestry of colors.
長瀞溪谷.desc:
Nagatoro Keikoku is a designated special scenic area, carved over the years by the Arakawa River into stunning schist cliffs. From mid to late November, thousands of maple and zelkova trees surrounding the steep gray cliffs transform into a breathtaking array of colors, painting the entire valley in vivid hues.

The most striking experience here is riding the "Nagatoro Arakawa Line Traditional Hand-Pulled Boat." Travelers can board a vintage wooden flat-bottom boat and glide into the mountains while listening to the boatman’s enchanting folk songs. On the right, the tranquil emerald stream flows gently, while on the left, vibrant forests cascade down the steep cliffs.

The main viewpoints and walking trails are well-planned and easy to navigate, but paths near the cliffs are often natural rocky terraces. It is highly recommended to wear non-slip casual shoes for maple viewing. Visiting on weekday mornings helps avoid crowds, making it perfect for capturing stunning reflections in photos.

中津峽.access_train: About 60 minutes by taxi from Seibu Chichibu Station on the Seibu Chichibu Line
中津峽.access_car: About 80 minutes from Hanazono IC on the Kanetsu Expressway
中津峽.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of Nakatsu Gorge's vibrant autumn colors and stunning new bridge.
中津峽.desc:
Nakatsu Gorge is a stunning chasm, over 100 meters deep, carved by the Nakatsu River in the Chichibu region. From late October to early November, thousands of maple and beech trees along the 10-kilometer rugged cliffs transform into a vibrant tapestry of colors, painting the entire mountainous gorge in a spectacular display.

The most striking visual moment occurs at the "Red Nakatsu River Bridge framed by a colorful floral carpet." From the high observation deck, the majestic crimson iron bridge spans the colorful cliffs and streams below, harmonizing beautifully with the red and yellow hues of the surrounding trees, creating a grand atmosphere.

As the main viewing paths are located along the deep rocky gorge roads, be sure to stay alert for passing vehicles while walking and searching for the perfect photo angles. This hidden gem remains relatively tranquil, making it a peaceful escape from the crowds. Aim to visit around noon when sunlight floods the valley, enhancing the vibrancy of the autumn leaves.

三峯神社.count: abundant autumn leaves throughout the shrine and approach
三峯神社.access_train: About 75 minutes by bus from Seibu Chichibu Station on the Seibu Chichibu Line
三峯神社.access_car: Approximately 90 minutes by car from Hanazono IC on the Kanetsu Expressway
三峯神社.tagline: Experience the sacred beauty of autumn at Mitsumine Shrine, where nature and tradition intertwine.
三峯神社.desc:
Mitsumine Shrine, perched at an elevation of 1,100 meters in the deep mountains, is a world-class ancient shrine that celebrates nature worship and rich history. Every early November, the shrine's majestic three torii gates, vibrant sacred gates, and surrounding temple grounds are adorned with hundreds of maple trees quietly turning red, painting the sacred realm in lush autumn hues.

The most representative aspect of Japanese aesthetics here lies in the dialogue between the colorful old shrine, ancient torii gates, and the historical ambiance of the maple forest. The vibrant foliage embraces rustic stone lanterns and towering ancient trees, creating a serene atmosphere, especially when the afternoon mist drifts through the mountains, allowing the sea of maples to embody the unique tranquility of this ancient temple.

To reach the main hall, visitors must navigate a natural gravel path and some steps, so wearing comfortable, non-slip sports shoes is recommended. During the autumn foliage season, shrine staff offer limited-edition, collectible amulets and goshuin (temple stamps). Visiting on weekday mornings is ideal, as there are fewer worshippers, making it the perfect time for capturing stunning traditional scenery.

飯能（Moomin Valley Park）.access_train: About 10 minutes by bus from Seibu Ikebukuro Line "Hanno Station"
飯能（Moomin Valley Park）.access_car: Approximately 15 minutes from Ken-O Expressway "Sayama Hidaka IC"
飯能（Moomin Valley Park）.tagline: Stroll through a Nordic fairy tale forest by Lake Miyazawa, capturing Moomin's cozy cabin and vibrant autumn leaves.
飯能（Moomin Valley Park）.desc:
Moomin Valley Park, located by Lake Miyazawa in Hanno City, is a magnificent theme park that perfectly recreates the charm of Nordic fairy tales. From mid to late November, the expansive coniferous forests and maple trees surrounding the lake reach their peak, adorning the entire European-style town with a vibrant tapestry of colors and life.

The park's core visual focus is the "interplay of the retro Moomin cabin, the lake, and the colorful forest scenery." A series of richly colored autumn leaves stretch along the gentle wooden promenade by the water. When the calm surface of the lake perfectly reflects the Nordic-style wooden architecture and the colorful autumn hues, the space feels vast and filled with the wild beauty of nature.

Entry to the park is ticketed, and the pathways are fully accessible, making it easy for families with elderly members or strollers to enjoy a leisurely stroll. The spacious lawns and shaded seating areas within the park are perfect for free-spirited travelers who appreciate a slow pace, especially on tranquil weekday mornings.

養老溪谷.address: 千葉縣夷隅郡大多喜町粟又
養老溪谷.access_train: About a 15-minute walk or taxi ride from Yoro Keikoku Station on the Kominato Railway
養老溪谷.access_car: Approximately a 50-minute drive from Ichihara IC on the Tateyama Expressway
養老溪谷.tagline: Discover Boso Peninsula's premier riverside trail, capturing the beauty of Awa-uta Falls and the vibrant autumn leaves at Kurokawa's grand torii.
養老溪谷.desc:
Yoro Keikoku is a renowned riverside destination, sculpted by the Yoro River over the years, celebrated for its stunning autumn foliage. Due to its location in the southern Boso Plain's climate, the blooming period here is the latest in Honshu, peaking from late November to early December, painting the entire pristine valley in a riot of colors.

The highlight is the several-kilometer-long 'riverside lowland cliff walkway.' The colorful forest stretches along the gentle riverbank and gravel paths, where the tranquil water perfectly reflects the famous tiered Awa-uta Falls and the vibrant foliage, creating an expansive scene that exudes elegant Zen vibes.

The area is extensive, with well-maintained flower viewing routes and wooden walkways, making it easy and comfortable to explore. However, some riverbed stones may be slippery, so wearing good non-slip running shoes is recommended. The contrast between the lush green leaves and autumn colors is even more striking on rainy days, making it an ideal spot for ecological photography, regardless of the weather.

成田山公園.access_train: About a 15-minute walk from Narita Station on the JR or Keisei Line
成田山公園.access_car: Approximately a 20-minute drive from Narita IC on the Higashi-Kanto Expressway
成田山公園.tagline: Discover the vibrant autumn hues of Naritasan Park, where tranquility meets tradition.
成田山公園.desc:
Naritasan Park is a stunning, traditional Japanese stroll garden spanning 160,000 square meters, located just behind the historic Naritasan Shinshoji Temple. Every year, from mid-November to late November, the park comes alive as over 250 ancient maple trees and ginkgo groves reach their peak, transforming this sacred space into a colorful spectacle.

The visual centerpiece features a classic pond, ancient stone lanterns, and the vibrant autumn leaves creating a mirror-like interplay. The colorful foliage lines the gentle gravel paths, and when the calm waters of "Monju Pond" perfectly reflect the red fountain and the vibrant canopy, the scene opens up, exuding an elegant Eastern Zen atmosphere.

The park's well-planned layout and gentle terrain make it ideal for families to stroll and savor history together. During the autumn foliage festival, traditional Japanese koto and shakuhachi performances are often held on weekends, drawing large crowds. For the best experience, visit on weekday mornings when the park is less crowded, perfect for capturing stunning reflections.

清水公園.access_train: Approximately a 3-minute walk from 清水公園 Station on the Tobu Noda Line
清水公園.access_car: About a 20-minute drive from the Joban Expressway 流山 IC
清水公園.tagline: Step into Japan's first private natural oasis, where ancient temples meet vibrant autumn leaves.
清水公園.desc:
清水公園 is a unique blend of rich natural ecology, cultural heritage, and large outdoor adventure facilities, making it Japan's first private park. Every year from mid to late November, hundreds of maple and zelkova trees surrounding the historic 金乗院 temple in the northern part of the park burst into vivid colors.

The park's visual highlight is the classic framing of the sturdy wooden sanmon gate, the Nio gate, and the maple forest. The vibrant foliage stretches along both sides of the long gravel path, beautifully contrasting with ancient stone lanterns. This picturesque scene perfectly embodies the tranquil historical Zen essence and the passage of time unique to the temple.

The park offers free admission (some facilities may charge), and its flat, well-paved grounds are very stroller and elderly-friendly. There is a large grassy plaza within the park, making it an ideal spot for a comfortable picnic while enjoying the autumn foliage. Visiting on weekday mornings ensures fewer crowds, perfect for capturing stunning cultural landscapes.

本土寺.access_train: 10-minute walk from JR Joban Line 'Kita-Kogane Station'
本土寺.access_car: about 10 minutes by car from the Joban Expressway 'Nagareyama IC'
本土寺.tagline: Visit the 'Maple Temple' in Matsudo City, where the five-story pagoda meets a thousand vibrant Iroha maples.
本土寺.desc:
Hondoji is a renowned ancient temple with over 700 years of history, belonging to the Nichiren sect. It's affectionately known as the 'Maple Temple' due to the abundance of flowers planted throughout its grounds. From late November to early December, the vast area bursts into color with over a thousand Iroha maples, creating a sacred and serene atmosphere.

The most iconic view features the perfect blend of the 'crimson five-story pagoda, the vintage wooden sanmon gate, and the maple forest.' The vibrant foliage stretches along the ancient stone-paved approach, harmonizing beautifully with the moss and historic stone markers. This interplay of movement and stillness perfectly embodies the unique tranquility of the temple.

The temple grounds are flat and compact, making it easy to explore. A visit and leisurely stroll will take about forty minutes. During the flower season, the temple collects a small donation for entry and offers limited edition omamori. Early mornings on weekdays provide the most ethereal atmosphere, allowing for a clean environment and excellent photo opportunities.

千葉公園.count: numerous colorful leaves throughout the park
千葉公園.address: 千葉縣千葉市中央区弁天3-1
千葉公園.access_train: About a 15-minute walk from JR Chiba Station
千葉公園.access_car: Approximately a 10-minute drive from Keiyo Road Anagawa IC
千葉公園.tagline: Discover the elegant dance of city maples and serene waters at Chiba Park.
千葉公園.desc:
Chiba Park is a quintessential urban oasis in Chiba City, showcasing exquisite landscapes and a relaxing atmosphere for locals. From mid-November to late November, the maple trees surrounding the expansive Watauta Pond reach their peak, with hundreds of vibrant leaves bursting into color, painting the city in rich autumn hues.

The visual highlight is the "harmonious interplay of pond shores, rowboats, and colorful forests." The vivid foliage blooms along the wooden pathways by the water, and as the calm surface perfectly reflects the colorful tree shadows and the distant city skyline, the space feels vast and refreshing, exuding a unique early autumn charm.

Located close to the city center and with completely flat terrain, the park is perfect for travelers seeking a leisurely morning stroll. You can rent rowboats to glide across the lake, enjoying the colorful forests from the water's perspective. Set aside an hour for a walk; weekday mornings are the least crowded, making it ideal for capturing stunning reflections.

六義園.count: approximately 70 maple trees in the garden
六義園.access_train: About a 7-minute walk from JR Yamanote Line "Komagome Station"
六義園.access_car: Limited parking nearby; taking the train is recommended.
六義園.tagline: Step into a stunning Edo-period garden, where vibrant autumn leaves and serene waters await.
六義園.desc:
Rikugien is a quintessential Edo-period garden, crafted over seven years by Yanagisawa Yoshiyasu, perfectly embodying the essence of Waka poetry.

Every year from late November to early December, hundreds of vibrant Iroha maple trees and a canopy of colorful leaves transform this sacred space into a lively spectacle.

The visual highlight is the "exquisite classical pond, Taiko stone bridge, and the mirrored interplay of autumn leaves." The colorful foliage lines the gentle gravel paths, and when the tranquil surface of "Oizumiko" reflects the vermilion of the Togetsukyo Bridge and the vibrant canopy, the scene exudes an elegant Zen atmosphere.

The garden features flat terrain and well-maintained paths, making it very accessible for seniors and strollers. During the autumn foliage festival, the famous nighttime illumination enhances the ethereal reflections. For the best experience, visit on a weekday morning right when the gates open to capture the pristine Japanese landscape.

小石川後樂園.access_train: About a 3-minute walk from Iidabashi Station on the Toei Oedo Line
小石川後樂園.access_car: Limited parking nearby; taking the train is recommended.
小石川後樂園.tagline: Discover a historic garden where nature and culture intertwine in vibrant autumn hues.
小石川後樂園.desc:
Koishikawa Kōrakuen is a special historic garden built by the Mito Tokugawa family, with design contributions from Shun Sui. Each year, from late November to early December, hundreds of ancient maple trees surrounding the expansive pond reach their peak foliage, adorning this historical sanctuary with classic elegance.

The most iconic scene features the "crimson Tsuten Bridge, the Tokugawa relic, and a tapestry of vibrant autumn leaves" in a timeless dialogue. The rustic stone pathways, enveloped by pink and bright red foliage, harmonize beautifully with the "Seiko Tei," a structure inspired by West Lake, perfectly embodying the refined horticultural aesthetics of the ancient capital.

The park's gently undulating terrain and well-maintained walking paths make for a comfortable stroll. Located near the bustling Tokyo Dome area, it attracts many visitors during the day. It is highly recommended to visit early on weekdays right after opening, allowing you to capture the essence of this cultural landscape amidst the historic buildings and colorful foliage.

奧多摩湖.count: extensive autumn foliage around the lakeside
奧多摩湖.address: 東京都西多摩郡奥多摩町原5
奧多摩湖.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from JR青梅線 (Ome Line) 奥多摩駅 (Okutama Station) or take a bus to 奥多摩湖 (Okutama Lake)
奧多摩湖.access_car: Approximately a 50-minute drive from the 中央自動車道 (Chuo Expressway) 上野原 IC (Uenohara IC)
奧多摩湖.tagline: Discover the enchanting 奥多摩湖, where vibrant autumn colors meet serene waters and majestic mountains.
奧多摩湖.desc:
奥多摩湖 (Okutama Lake), also known as the 小河內水庫 (Kokaochi Reservoir), is a vast artificial dam lake straddling Tokyo and Yamanashi Prefecture, formed by the 小河內大壩 (Kokaochi Dam) blocking the 多摩川 (Tama River). From late October to mid-November each year, the expansive beech and maple forests surrounding the lake reach their peak, painting this suburban green oasis in a riot of colors.

The most iconic sight here is the "麦山浮橋 (Mugiyama Floating Bridge) and the dynamic interplay of autumn leaves reflected on the lake's surface." The unique floating bridge gently sways over the emerald waters, and when the calm surface perfectly mirrors the towering 奥多摩連峰 (Okutama Mountains) and the lush colorful forests, the scene feels expansive and refreshingly cool.

The lakeside walking trails and waterfront wooden paths are well-maintained, making it ideal for independent travelers seeking a leisurely morning hike or a scenic drive. Due to the strong winds and cool temperatures in the mornings and evenings at this high-altitude lake, it's essential to bring a warm jacket for outdoor photography. Visiting on a clear, windless morning offers the best chance to capture stunning reflections.

鎌倉（鶴岡八幡宮）.address: 神奈川縣鎌倉市雪ノ下2-1-31
鎌倉（鶴岡八幡宮）.access_train: 15-minute walk from JR Yokosuka Line "Kamakura Station"
鎌倉（鶴岡八幡宮）.access_car: approximately 15 minutes by car from Yokohama-Yokosuka Road "Asahina IC"
鎌倉（鶴岡八幡宮）.tagline: Discover the sacred beauty of Kamakura, where autumn leaves ignite the ancient grounds of Tsurugaoka Hachimangu.
鎌倉（鶴岡八幡宮）.desc:
Tsurugaoka Hachimangu is an ancient shrine with nearly a thousand years of history, ranking among the top three shrines in Kamakura. From late November to early December, hundreds of maple and cherry trees surrounding the majestic torii gate, main hall, and both sides of the approach turn vibrant shades of red, adding a splash of early autumn color to this sacred and solemn domain.

The core feature is the classic scenery of "crimson shrine, ancient moss, and autumn leaves." The colorful foliage embraces the rustic stone lanterns and towering ancient trees, especially around the "Yanagihara Shinike" and Genji Pond, where the calm water reflects the vibrant colors, perfectly embodying the tranquil Zen spirit unique to this historic site.

The path to the main hall is completely flat, with well-maintained gravel walkways that are very friendly for elderly visitors and strollers. To avoid the crowds and capture the most pristine and delicate ancient scenery, it is highly recommended to arrive before 8 AM on weekdays. Limited edition omamori are available during the flower season.

丹澤湖.count: vast red-leaf forest around the lake
丹澤湖.address: 神奈川縣足柄上郡山北町中川
丹澤湖.access_train: About 30 minutes by taxi from JR Gotemba Line "Yamabuk Station"
丹澤湖.access_car: Approximately 40 minutes from Tomei Expressway "Ooi Matsuda IC"
丹澤湖.tagline: Discover breathtaking autumn hues at the serene shores of Lake Tanzawa.
丹澤湖.desc:
Lake Tanzawa is a vast artificial reservoir nestled at the foot of Tanzawa Mountain, recognized as one of Kanagawa's top 50 scenic spots. Every mid-November, the gentle lakeside trails and surrounding parks come alive with vibrant maple and beech trees, painting the entire area in a stunning array of colors.

The visual highlight is the "crimson bridge, emerald mountains, and a mirror-like sea of colorful foliage." The vibrant forest stretches alongside the clear lake, and when the tranquil water perfectly reflects the iconic cable-stayed "Eisai Bridge" and the colorful trees, the scene opens up to reveal nature's pure beauty.

The lakeside trail is well-maintained and easy to navigate, making it perfect for travelers seeking a leisurely morning stroll or a bike ride. Located in a valley, it's highly recommended to bring a sunshade and visit in the early morning to capture the most perfect reflections of the colorful foliage on the water's surface.

三溪園.address: 神奈川縣橫浜市中区本牧三之谷58-1
三溪園.access_train: About 10 minutes by bus from JR and municipal subway "Negishi Station" to "Sankei-en Mae"
三溪園.access_car: About 10 minutes by car from the Shuto Expressway "Ishikawacho IC"
三溪園.tagline: Step into Yokohama's nationally designated garden and capture the ancient pagoda's reflection among vibrant autumn leaves.
三溪園.desc:
Created by entrepreneur Hara Sankei, this expansive garden is a point of pride for Yokohama as a nationally designated scenic spot. From late November to early December, the historic buildings and traditional houses relocated from Kyoto and Kamakura are surrounded by hundreds of maple trees and sycamores, all transforming into a stunning display of deep Eastern aesthetics.

The most iconic view here is undoubtedly the "Old Tomyoji Three-Story Pagoda framed by the maple forest." Vivid autumn foliage embraces ancient moss-covered wooden structures and the expansive pond's surface, creating a scene reminiscent of an ink wash painting, especially when enveloped in mist.

The garden's vast grounds are rich with historical relics, so it's recommended to set aside at least 1.5 hours to explore leisurely. Visiting on a weekday morning is highly suggested to fully enjoy the serene atmosphere and perfect lighting for photography.

大山寺.count: numerous autumn leaves along the path
大山寺.address: 神奈川縣伊勢原市大山724
大山寺.access_train: Take the Odakyu Line to Isehara Station, then a bus for about 30 minutes to "Oyama Cable" and ride the cable car.
大山寺.access_car: About a 30-minute drive from the Tomei Expressway "Atsugi IC."
大山寺.tagline: Challenge the long stone steps to witness a breathtaking tunnel of autumn leaves and enchanting night illuminations.
大山寺.desc:
Daiyama-ji, nestled in the foothills of Mount Oyama in Isehara City, is a historically rich flower temple belonging to the Shingon sect of Daikaku-ji. Every year, from mid to late November, the long stone steps leading to the main hall are flanked by hundreds of vibrant Iroha maple trees, earning it the affectionate title of Kanto's top destination for autumn leaves.

The visual highlights include the "dialogue between stone steps, moss-covered statues, and the vivid colors of the forest." As you ascend the ancient, winding gravel steps, the lush, colorful walls of foliage rise nearly to eye level. During the autumn festival, the famous dreamlike illuminations transform the entire mountain into a golden wonderland.

While you can take the Oyama Mountain Cable Car to Daiyama-ji Station, the path from the station still has a noticeable incline and stone steps, so sturdy, non-slip hiking shoes are highly recommended. Expect large crowds during the night illuminations; for a chance to capture the perfect, serene shot of the red-leafed corridor, it's best to visit on a weekday morning.

彌彥公園.count: about 300 maple trees, especially spectacular during the illumination period
彌彥公園.address: 新潟縣西蒲原郡彌彥村彌彥
彌彥公園.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from Mihon Station on the Mihon Line
彌彥公園.access_car: Approximately a 30-minute drive from Sanjo Tsubame IC on the Hokuriku Expressway
彌彥公園.tagline: Stroll through Echigo's green oasis, marvel at the crimson Tsukimi Bridge and 16 hectares of vibrant autumn leaves.
彌彥公園.desc:
Mihon Park, adjacent to the historic Mihon Shrine, spans an impressive 16 hectares, blending Japanese tradition with the beauty of nature. From late October to mid-November, the park's renowned "Momiji Valley" reaches its peak foliage, showcasing hundreds of ancient maple trees and vibrant forests in full bloom.

The main visual highlight is the perfect harmony of the "crimson Tsukimi Bridge and the colorful flower carpet." This vintage wooden drum bridge spans a clear stream, with a cascading display of red and yellow foliage beneath it. When illuminated at night, the enchanting light and shadow dance across the flower beds, revealing the ultimate essence of Eastern historical tranquility.

The park features gentle terrain and well-maintained paths, making it ideal for families to leisurely explore while savoring history. The main walking routes are very stroller and elder-friendly. Set aside an hour for a stroll, especially in the early morning on weekdays when crowds are minimal, perfect for capturing stunning reflections.

奧只見湖.count: The lake's surface reflects the autumn leaves spectacularly.
奧只見湖.fee: 免費（遊覽船大人 1,400 円）
奧只見湖.address: 新潟縣魚沼市湯之谷芋川
奧只見湖.access_train: About 75 minutes by bus from JR Joetsu Line "Koide Station."
奧只見湖.access_car: Approximately 60 minutes from Kanetsu Expressway "Koide IC."
奧只見湖.tagline: Explore Japan's largest artificial lake and capture the beauty of golden beech trees in a hidden paradise.
奧只見湖.desc:
奧只見湖 is a vast artificial dam lake straddling Niigata and Fukushima, recognized as one of Japan's hidden gems. From mid to late October, the expansive beech and maple forests surrounding the lake reach their peak, transforming the deep blue waters into a vibrant tapestry of colors.

The highlight here is the stunning juxtaposition of the pristine mountain scenery and the classic retro sightseeing boats. As you drive slowly along the scenic road, golden fields stretch out on either side, and taking a ride on the "奧只見湖遊覽船" allows you to admire the colorful floral walls cascading down the shores, framed by the majestic Echigo mountain range in the background, creating a sense of vastness.

Due to its high-altitude valley location, temperatures in mid-October can drop close to single digits, so be sure to bring a warm coat and a windproof hat for your maple-viewing adventure. The road will close for winter in early November due to snow, so check the official website for the latest flower conditions. Aim for a clear, windless morning for the best chance to capture stunning mirror-like reflections.

苗場.count: Vast autumn foliage along the Dragondola route and at the summit
苗場.address: 新潟縣南魚沼郡湯沢町三国
苗場.access_train: About 30 minutes by bus from JR Joetsu Shinkansen "Echigo-Yuzawa Station" to "苗場スキー場"
苗場.access_car: About 30 minutes from Kanetsu Expressway "Yuzawa IC"
苗場.tagline: Ride Japan's longest gondola and gaze upon a breathtaking 5.5-kilometer tapestry of autumn leaves.
苗場.desc:
苗場高原 is a stunning resort nestled among natural hills, beautiful in every season. From mid-October to early November, the once green slopes transform into a vibrant sea of colors, as thousands of maple and beech trees change hues, painting the entire pristine forest in a kaleidoscope of shades.

The ultimate visual experience here is riding the 5,481-meter-long "苗場 Dragondola," renowned as Japan's longest gondola. Travelers can ascend slowly, feeling the refreshing highland breeze, and enjoy a bird's-eye view of the valley below, where the colorful carpet of yellow and red stretches infinitely, creating a powerful visual impact.

Due to its high-altitude location, the temperature here is cooler than at lower elevations, making it an ideal spot for early autumn leaf-peeping. With the open highland terrain, be sure to secure your hat while taking photos. The one-way ride lasts up to 25 minutes, and morning departures offer the best light reflecting off the surface of 二居湖, enhancing the colors.

高田城址公園.count: extensive colorful foliage throughout the park
高田城址公園.access_train: About 20 minutes by bus or taxi from JR Hokuriku Shinkansen "Jōetsu Myōkō Station"
高田城址公園.access_car: About 20 minutes from Hokuriku Expressway "Jōetsu IC"
高田城址公園.tagline: Experience the fiery hues of autumn by the historic Takada Castle ruins.
高田城址公園.desc:
Takada Jōshi Kōen is built on the former site of Takada Castle, once the stronghold of the Takada clan during the Edo period, and is renowned nationwide for its stunning night cherry blossoms. From late October to early November, hundreds of maple trees in the park turn vibrant red, beautifully contrasting against the imposing ancient castle walls and the restored wooden castle tower.

The park's core feature is its "retro castle moat, striking castle tower, and classical views of autumn leaves." The colorful foliage blooms alongside the gentle waterway, and when the tranquil surface perfectly reflects the white walls and tiled roofs of the "Gokuraku Bridge" along with the vibrant forest, it creates a spacious scene that showcases elegant Japanese aesthetics.

The park's terrain is completely flat, and the ancient pathways are well-maintained, making it very friendly for seniors and strollers. During the autumn foliage festival, local cultural events are often held on weekends. Set aside an hour for a leisurely stroll; early mornings have the fewest crowds, making it the perfect time for photography and capturing that ideal mirror-like reflection.

昇仙峽.count: 4 kilometers of gorge lined with abundant autumn leaves
昇仙峽.access_train: Take a bus from JR Kofu Station for about 40 minutes to Shosenkyo-guchi.
昇仙峽.access_car: Approximately a 30-minute drive from Chuo Expressway Kofu Showa IC.
昇仙峽.tagline: Experience breathtaking beauty where vibrant autumn leaves meet majestic cliffs!
昇仙峽.desc:
Miyake Shosenkyo is celebrated as "Japan's most beautiful gorge," featuring a stunning granite cliff canyon designated as a special scenic spot. From mid-October to late November, the dramatic elevation changes allow the autumn leaves to cascade down from the upper reaches, painting the steep cliffs in a vibrant tapestry of colors.

The most visually striking moment occurs when the solitary Jakuan Peak and the 30-meter-high Sen'na Falls engage in a stunning dialogue with the fiery maple reflections. Standing on the Tenkou Bridge, you can witness the rushing clear stream creating white splashes as it weaves through the golden canopies. The towering cliffs, reaching heights of 180 meters, frame the colorful foliage, creating a breathtaking spectacle.

The gorge's walking paths are well-maintained and easy to navigate, making it perfect for a leisurely family hike along the riverside. For the best experience, visit between 10 AM and noon when the sunlight directly illuminates the valley floor, showcasing the most vibrant autumn colors, ideal for landscape photography.

山中湖.count: Numerous autumn leaves around the lakeside and in Hanano Sato Park
山中湖.address: 山梨縣南都留郡山中湖村山中
山中湖.access_train: Take the JR from "Otsuki Station" to "Fujisan Station" and then a bus for about 30 minutes
山中湖.access_car: Approximately a 20-minute drive from the Tomei Expressway "Gotemba IC"
山中湖.tagline: Experience the breathtaking autumn hues by the serene shores of Lake Yamanakako, where nature's beauty meets Fuji's majesty.
山中湖.desc:
Yamanakako is the largest and highest natural dam lake among the Fuji Five Lakes. From mid-October to mid-November, the "Maple Romance Street" along the shores of Asahi Hill comes alive, showcasing hundreds of maple trees and their vibrant colors, transforming this natural wonder into a kaleidoscope of hues.

The most iconic sight here is the stunning interplay of "majestic Mount Fuji, the tranquil lakeside, and the mirrored autumn leaves." Strips of red and yellow foliage stretch along the calm waters, and when the clear surface perfectly reflects Mount Fuji and the lush, colorful trees, the scene opens up, revealing a dynamic beauty that captivates the soul.

The lakeside road is well-planned, featuring comfortable wooden walkways that are perfect for a leisurely bike ride. During the autumn festival, enchanting night illuminations enhance the reflections, creating an ethereal atmosphere. For the best photography, visit on a clear, windless morning to capture the perfect reflection of the colorful foliage on the lake's surface.

清里高原.count: extensive autumn foliage across the highlands
清里高原.address: 山梨縣北杜市高根町清里
清里高原.access_train: A short walk from JR Koumi Line "Kiyosato Station"
清里高原.access_car: Approximately 30 minutes drive from Chuo Expressway "Nagakubo IC"
清里高原.tagline: A European-style summer oasis at the foot of the Yatsugatake, where vibrant autumn colors dazzle the landscape.
清里高原.desc:
Kiyosato Kogen is nestled on the expansive slopes of Yatsugatake, reaching an elevation of 1,200 meters, making it a popular getaway filled with fresh, open-air vibes. From mid-October to early November, the beech and maple trees surrounding the highland valleys reach their peak, transforming the entire pristine forest trails into a colorful spectacle.

The most breathtaking visual highlight is the "crimson Azumaya Bridge framed by the majestic peak of Aka-dake in the distance." Standing at either Kiyosato Kogen Observatory or Seisenryo, you can gaze at the magnificent 138-meter arch bridge spanning the deep Kawamata River gorge, with a stunning expanse of vibrant yellow and red foliage stretching for kilometers beneath it, creating a grand atmosphere.

The open terrain of the highlands features well-maintained, flat walking paths, making it perfect for independent travelers looking for a leisurely hike or a scenic drive. Due to the high altitude, the mornings and evenings can be breezy and slightly cool, so be sure to carry a warm jacket for outdoor photography. Visiting on weekday mornings allows you to fully enjoy the ethereal tranquility of the mountains.

西湖療癒之里根場.count: autumn leaves around the thatched-roof village
西湖療癒之里根場.address: 山梨縣南都留郡富士河口湖町西湖根場2710
西湖療癒之里根場.access_train: Take the JR to Otsuki Station, transfer to Fujikyu to Kawaguchiko Station, then a 40-minute bus ride.
西湖療癒之里根場.access_car: About a 30-minute drive from the Chuo Expressway's Kawaguchiko IC.
西湖療癒之里根場.tagline: Step into a traditional thatched-roof village by Lake Sai and capture the timeless beauty of autumn leaves against Mount Fuji.
西湖療癒之里根場.desc:
Nemba, the Healing Village by Lake Sai, perfectly replicates the historical preservation of Japan's traditional "kado-zukuri" thatched-roof homes. From late October to early November, the maple trees lining the village and the slopes behind transform into vibrant autumn hues, painting the serene valley with rich early fall colors.

The most iconic scene features the classic composition of traditional thatched-roof houses, a flowing stream, and the autumn foliage of Mount Fuji. Rustic wooden structures are scattered harmoniously, complemented by the colorful forest at your feet and charming bridges over the stream. With the majestic Mount Fuji towering in the background, it feels as if you've stepped into a picturesque Japanese landscape.

The village is well-planned, with smooth gravel paths and various thatched houses housing cultural exhibits, craft experiences, and historical artifacts. Compared to Kawaguchiko, this area offers a more tranquil experience for visitors. Arriving early on weekdays, just before dawn, allows you to enjoy pristine street views and exceptional lighting for photography.

輕井澤（雲場池）.count: The reflections on the lake's surface combined with the autumn leaves create a breathtaking scene.
輕井澤（雲場池）.address: 長野縣北佐久郡輕井澤町輕井澤
輕井澤（雲場池）.access_train: About a 15-minute walk from JR Hokuriku Shinkansen 'Karuizawa Station.'
輕井澤（雲場池）.access_car: Approximately a 10-minute drive from the Joshinetsu Expressway 'Karuizawa IC.'
輕井澤（雲場池）.tagline: A serene summer oasis at the foot of Asama, where you can savor the beauty of 'Swan Lake' and vibrant autumn leaves.
輕井澤（雲場池）.desc:
Yumura-ike, nestled in the historic resort town of Karuizawa, is affectionately known as 'Swan Lake' due to its resemblance to the habitat of swans. From mid-October to early November each year, the maple and larch trees lining the narrow lakeshore reach their peak, transforming this tranquil sanctuary into a vibrant tapestry of colors and life.

The core visual feature is the interplay between the 'long, mirror-like water surface and the vibrant forest.' Striking reds and oranges of the foliage bloom alongside the gentle gravel paths. When the calm, mirror-like water perfectly reflects the blue sky and the closely growing larches and maples, the scene feels expansive and exudes refined elegance.

The lakeside walking path is completely flat and well-maintained, taking about twenty minutes to stroll around, making it very friendly for seniors and strollers. As a world-class hotspot, it often attracts large groups of tourists during the day. Experienced photographers strongly recommend visiting on weekday mornings for the best chance to capture stunning mirror-like landscapes.

戶隱.count: Abundant autumn leaves around Togakushi Shrine and along the Okusha path
戶隱.access_train: About 60 minutes by bus from JR Nagano Station to Togakushi
戶隱.access_car: Approximately 40 minutes from Joshinetsu Expressway Nagano IC
戶隱.tagline: Discover the sacred realm of Kurozun Shrine at the foot of Togakushi, where ancient trees meet vibrant autumn leaves.
戶隱.desc:
Togakushi has long been a spiritual site for Shugendo, renowned for its five Togakushi Shrines steeped in history. Every year, from early to late October, the majestic torii gates and ancient shrine buildings are surrounded by pristine forests that transform into a sea of autumn colors, creating a serene and sacred atmosphere.

The most iconic view features the striking vermilion Suwa Shrine Gate, thatched roofs, and the ancient cedar forest in a classic dialogue. The vibrant foliage embraces rustic stone lanterns and towering trees, and as you stroll along the two-kilometer path to the Okusha Shrine, the interplay of sound and silence perfectly captures the tranquility unique to this ancient temple.

The trail from Suwa Shrine Gate to Okusha is a long, natural gravel slope, so it's highly recommended to wear hiking shoes with good grip and support. Mornings in the deep mountain area can be foggy, and the ethereal beauty of stone Buddhas and colorful trees amidst the mist offers the best photo opportunities, away from the crowds.

鏡池.count: a stunning view of the pond reflecting the Togakushi mountains and autumn leaves
鏡池.address: 長野縣長野市戶隱中社
鏡池.access_train: Take a bus from JR Nagano Station for about 60 minutes to "Kagami Ike Entrance" and walk from there.
鏡池.access_car: About a 40-minute drive from the Joshinetsu Expressway "Nagano IC."
鏡池.tagline: Discover a hidden gem where the majestic peaks reflect in crystal-clear waters.
鏡池.desc:
Kagami Ike, nestled deep within the 1,000-meter-high Togakushi Plateau, is a compact yet renowned natural dam volcanic pond in Hokuriku. Every year, from mid-October, the surrounding Japanese beech and maple trees burst into vibrant autumn colors, transforming this natural wonder into a photographer's paradise.

The breathtaking spectacle here is the "perfect reflection of the rugged Togakushi mountain range against the glassy water surface." Standing on the specially designed wooden walkway, you'll be captivated by the mirror-like, undisturbed waters that flawlessly capture the imposing gray cliffs behind and the lush, colorful foliage in front, creating a grand visual experience.

The pond area features well-maintained, gentle walking paths and a charming retro café, making it easy to explore. Since the winds are calmest in the early morning, it's highly recommended to visit on a clear, windless morning. At this time, sunlight filters through the treetops, illuminating the colorful leaves and creating an ethereal, meditative reflection in the air.

松本城.count: numerous colorful leaves in the castle site park
松本城.access_train: 15-minute walk from JR Shinonoi Line "Matsumoto Station"
松本城.access_car: About 10 minutes by car from Nagano Expressway "Matsumoto IC"
松本城.tagline: Experience Japan's oldest surviving five-story castle, surrounded by vibrant autumn foliage and serene moats.
松本城.desc:
Matsumoto Castle, built during the Eisho era, is one of Japan's four national treasures, renowned for its striking black and white "Crow Castle" design. From late October to early November, the hundreds of maple trees within the park turn a brilliant red, adding a splash of early autumn color to the majestic ancient castle walls.

The main attraction lies in the "unique black lacquered keep, vermilion gates, and the classical backdrop of autumn leaves." The colorful foliage blooms alongside the gentle waterways, and when the calm water perfectly reflects the towering national treasure, the distant snow-capped Northern Alps, and the vibrant forest, the scene opens up to showcase the ultimate in Japanese aesthetic beauty.

The park's terrain is completely flat, and the ancient pathways are well-maintained, making it very friendly for seniors and strollers. However, be cautious when visiting the steep stairs inside the national treasure keep, which have a maximum incline of 61 degrees. Early mornings on weekdays see the fewest crowds, making it the best time for photography and capturing that perfect reflection.

金澤城公園.count: numerous colorful leaves throughout the park
金澤城公園.fee: 免費（石川門・三十間長屋等建築 320 円）
金澤城公園.access_train: About 15 minutes by bus from JR Kanazawa Station to "Kenrokuen-shita/Kanazawa Castle"
金澤城公園.access_car: Approximately 20 minutes from Hokuriku Expressway "Kanazawa Nishi IC"
金澤城公園.tagline: Experience the majestic ruins of Kanazawa Castle, surrounded by a fiery sea of autumn leaves.
金澤城公園.desc:
Kanazawa Castle Park is built on the historic site of Kanazawa Castle, once home to the Maeda clan during the Edo period, and is adjacent to Kenrokuen Garden. From late October to mid-November, the park’s famous maple trees reach their peak, with hundreds of trees turning vibrant red, beautifully contrasting against the grand white tiled castle walls.

The park's core feature is its "classic scenery of the retro moat, sturdy stone walls, and autumn leaves." The colorful foliage blooms alongside the tranquil waterways, and when the calm water perfectly reflects the white walls and tiled roofs of the "Hishiyagura" tower along with the vibrant autumn colors, it creates a spacious scene that showcases elegant Japanese aesthetics and the legacy of the Maeda clan.

The park's terrain is completely flat, with well-maintained stone paths, making it very friendly for seniors and strollers. During the autumn foliage festival, a magical lighting event enhances the reflections, adding to the ethereal atmosphere. Visiting early in the morning on weekdays offers the least crowds, making it the perfect time to capture the beauty of this historic castle.

鶴仙溪.address: 石川縣加賀市山中溫泉
鶴仙溪.access_train: Take a bus from JR Hokuriku Main Line 'Kaga Onsen Station' for about 20 minutes to 'Yamanaka Onsen'
鶴仙溪.access_car: About a 20-minute drive from the Hokuriku Expressway 'Kaga IC'
鶴仙溪.tagline: Explore the serene Tsurugaseki Valley, where nature and hot springs meet in a vibrant tapestry of colors.
鶴仙溪.desc:
Tsurugaseki is a hidden gem along the Daishoji River, stretching 1.3 kilometers through a picturesque hot spring town, renowned as one of Hokuriku's top spots for autumn foliage. From mid-November to late November each year, the towering ancient forests and maple trees lining the old moat transform in unison, painting the deep valley in a stunning array of colors, bursting with life.

The most iconic visual experience is undoubtedly the harmonious blend of the rustic Kurumi Bridge, the modern Ayatori Bridge, and the valley's vibrant autumn leaves. Stroll along the well-maintained wooden path by the stream, where on your right, the tranquil green waters flow, and on your left, a colorful floral tapestry cascades down steep cliffs, merging modern lines with classical beauty.

The valley's walking trail is flat and well-constructed, making it perfect for a leisurely morning hike followed by a day of soaking in hot springs. Due to the limited width of the main viewing bridges and waterfront paths, please avoid blocking pathways for extended periods and refrain from using large tripods during photo shoots. Visiting on weekday mornings offers the brightest light filtering into the valley.

西山公園.access_train: About a 20-minute walk from JR Hokuriku Main Line 'Sabae Station'
西山公園.access_car: About a 5-minute drive from Hokuriku Expressway 'Sabae IC'
西山公園.tagline: Discover a stunning tapestry of 1,600 fiery red Iroha maples in this serene riverside oasis.
西山公園.desc:
Nishiyama Park is one of Japan's top urban parks, renowned as a premier maple viewing destination in the Hokuriku region. Every year from mid to late November, the park's 1,600 Iroha maples and maple trees transform in color, painting the entire hillside with vibrant hues and life.

The main visual highlight is the harmonious blend of a retro Japanese garden, a vermilion bridge, and a colorful floral carpet. As you stroll along the winding paths to the summit, you’ll be surrounded by a lush display of fiery red foliage. When the calm waters perfectly reflect the towering maple tunnel, the expansive scene showcases the pinnacle of horticultural beauty.

The park's vast grounds feature gentle slopes and well-maintained, spacious pathways, making it very accessible for seniors and families with strollers. There's also a free-entry Nishiyama Zoo, famous for its red pandas, making it a perfect spot for family outings. For a peaceful experience, visit in the early morning on weekdays when the crowds are minimal, ideal for leisurely walks and capturing stunning photos.

刈込池.count: autumn foliage and lake reflections in the depths of Hakusan
刈込池.hours: 依登山道（White Road 開放期間）
刈込池.fee: White Road 通行費（輕型車 1,760 円）
刈込池.address: 福井縣大野市南六呂師
刈込池.access_car: Approximately 60 minutes from the Chubu Jukan Expressway "Ono IC" (no public transport available).
刈込池.tagline: Discover a hidden gem where vibrant autumn colors mirror the majestic peaks of San-no-mine.
刈込池.desc:
刈込池 is a secluded volcanic pond nestled upstream of the Tabakawa River, known for its serene clarity and hidden beauty. Every year from mid to late October, the surrounding birch and Japanese beech trees reach their peak, transforming the ancient forest into a vibrant tapestry of colors, making it a photographer's ultimate secret spot.

The breathtaking spectacle here is the "pristine water surface reflecting the towering San-no-mine perfectly." Standing on the specially constructed wooden walkway, the glassy, ripple-free water captures the fiery hues of the autumn foliage and the azure sky, creating a stunning visual masterpiece.

To reach 刈込池, you’ll need to hike a one-hour trail from the Koike parking lot, which includes natural stone steps and steep inclines. For those coming to enjoy the autumn leaves, it’s highly recommended to wear professional hiking shoes and a warm, windproof jacket. Aim for a clear, windless morning for the best chance to witness the mirror-like scenery on the water.

養浩館庭園.count: exquisite autumn leaves in a pond-style strolling garden
養浩館庭園.access_train: About a 15-minute walk from JR Fukui Station
養浩館庭園.access_car: Approximately a 15-minute drive from the Hokuriku Expressway Fukui IC
養浩館庭園.tagline: Stroll through a historic garden of the Fukui clan, savoring the elegance of water houses and autumn leaves.
養浩館庭園.desc:
養浩館庭園 is a beautifully preserved garden that showcases the elegance of the Matsudaira family's Edo-period villa. It consistently ranks among the top gardens in American Japanese garden magazines. Every year from mid to late November, the maple trees surrounding the expansive pond come alive with vibrant autumn colors, adorning this historical sanctuary with classic beauty.

The visual highlight is the "timeless dialogue between the classic water house, the pond, and the autumn leaves." The rustic sukiya-style architecture appears to float on the water's surface. As visitors sit on tatami mats and gaze outward, the tranquil water perfectly reflects the pink and bright red foliage, creating a stunning interplay of stillness and movement that embodies refined Japanese aesthetics.

The garden's terrain is completely flat, and the ancient walking paths are well-maintained, making for a comfortable stroll. Located close to Fukui city, it’s incredibly convenient for independent travelers seeking a leisurely morning walk. Entering early on weekdays allows you to capture the stories woven into the wooden textures of the old buildings.

惠那峽.address: 岐阜縣恵那市大井町惠那峡
惠那峽.access_train: About 10 minutes by taxi from JR Chuo Line '恵那 Station'
惠那峽.access_car: About 10 minutes' drive from Chuo Expressway '恵那 IC'
惠那峽.tagline: Experience breathtaking views of giant cliffs and vibrant valleys on a Kiso River sightseeing boat ride.
惠那峽.desc:
惠那峽 is a nationally designated scenic spot formed by the Kiso River's relentless carving and the construction of the Oi Dam. From late October to mid-November each year, the steep gray cliffs on either side come alive as towering maple and zelkova trees reach their peak, painting the entire gorge in a riot of colors.  

The visual highlight is the dialogue between the 'strange rock formations, emerald green waters, and the vibrant hues of the forest.' A leisurely ride on the '惠那峽 sightseeing boat' allows you to see naturally formed umbrella rocks and red rocks weaving through the golden canopies, creating a stunning contrast with the clear water below. The expansive scene offers a perfect balance of strength and softness.  

The area is well-planned, with flat and accessible walking paths that are friendly for seniors and strollers alike. This classic destination is incredibly convenient for self-driving visitors. Aim to visit between 10 AM and noon, when the sunlight directly illuminates the valley, showcasing the most vibrant autumn foliage.

下呂溫泉.count: around the hot spring town and Gassho Village
下呂溫泉.access_train: A short walk from JR Takayama Line "Gero Station"
下呂溫泉.access_car: About a 30-minute drive from the Tokai-Hokuriku Expressway "Hida Kanayama IC"
下呂溫泉.tagline: Discover the enchanting beauty of Gero Onsen, where tradition meets vibrant autumn colors.
下呂溫泉.desc:
Gero Onsen, alongside Kusatsu and Arima, is one of Japan's three great hot spring towns, steeped in rich history. From early to mid-November, the nearby "Gero Onsen Gassho Village" and "Momiji no Sato" reach their peak, with hundreds of maple trees turning a brilliant red, adorning the sacred landscape with their beauty.

The most iconic scene features the "traditional thatched-roof houses, steaming hot springs, and the dialogue of autumn leaves." The colorful foliage embraces ancient stone lanterns, and as the enchanting night illuminations begin, the entire mountain transforms into a golden world, harmonizing beautifully with the tranquil waters, showcasing Eastern aesthetics and a dreamlike atmosphere.

The hot spring town and Gassho Village trails are well-maintained and perfect for a leisurely morning stroll or a day of soaking in the hot springs. As a world-class destination, it attracts many visitors during the day. It is highly recommended to visit on weekday mornings or during the evening illuminations for the most captivating and story-filled photographs.

飛驒高山.count: Vibrant foliage around Takayama Old Town and the Jinya
飛驒高山.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from JR Takayama Station
飛驒高山.access_car: Approximately a 45-minute drive from the Tokai-Hokuriku Expressway "Hida Kiyomi IC"
飛驒高山.tagline: Stroll through the traditional wooden streets of Miyagawa, capturing the fiery red bridge and the autumn foliage along the riverbank.
飛驒高山.desc:
Hida Takayama is affectionately known as "Little Kyoto of Hida," preserving a complete set of wooden buildings from the Edo period. From mid-October to mid-November each year, hundreds of maple trees around the ancient streets and along the Miyagawa River turn vibrant red, adding a splash of early autumn color to this grand old town.

The core feature here is the "red bridge, Edo-era architecture, and the classic backdrop of autumn leaves." The colorful foliage blooms alongside the gentle waterways, and when the calm surface of the Miyagawa perfectly reflects the vivid red bridge, the rustic wooden townhouses, and the vibrant forest, it creates an expansive scene that showcases the ultimate beauty of Japanese aesthetics.

The old streets are completely flat, and the ancient stone-paved pathways are well-maintained, making it very friendly for seniors and strollers. There are rich cultural exhibition halls and delicious Hida beef nearby, perfect for leisurely travelers. Early mornings on weekdays see the fewest crowds, making it ideal for capturing stunning cultural landscapes.

修善寺.count: Abundant autumn leaves along the bamboo path and Katsura River
修善寺.access_train: Take a bus from Izu Hakone Railway "Shuzenji Station" for about 7 minutes to "Shuzenji Onsen"
修善寺.access_car: Approximately 50 minutes by car from Tomei Expressway "Numazu IC"
修善寺.tagline: Discover Japan's oldest hot spring town, where bamboo groves, crimson bridges, and autumn leaves create a breathtaking escape.
修善寺.desc:
Shuzenji Onsen is listed among Japan's top hot springs and is renowned as Izu's oldest famous bath, shaped by the flowing waters of the Katsura River. Nestled in a warm valley, the autumn leaves here bloom later than elsewhere, transforming from mid-November to early December. The vibrant colors surround the ancient Shuzenji Temple and the maple trees by the river, creating a stunning tapestry of reds and golds.

The quintessential Japanese aesthetic is embodied in the "crimson Katsura Bridge, bamboo grove paths, and the classical dialogue of brilliant autumn leaves." Stroll along the well-maintained riverside pathways, where the lush green of the Moso bamboo contrasts beautifully with the cascading reds and yellows of the maple forest, all set against the backdrop of unique historical architecture.

The flat, well-kept paths of the hot spring town are very friendly for seniors and strollers. During the autumn foliage festival, the gardens of Shuzenji Temple offer special access, making it a valuable experience. Early mornings on weekdays are the best time for capturing the serene beauty of this historic setting with fewer visitors.

熱海梅園.count: The autumn foliage in the park has its own unique charm.
熱海梅園.fee: 免費（梅まつり期間有收費）
熱海梅園.access_train: Take a bus from JR Atami Station for about 20 minutes to "Baien".
熱海梅園.access_car: Approximately 20 minutes by car from Nishishō Bypass "Ishibashi IC".
熱海梅園.tagline: Step into Honshu's latest autumn wonderland, capturing early winter plum blossoms, rustic wooden bridges, and vibrant autumn foliage.
熱海梅園.desc:
Atami Baien is renowned for its stunning plum blossoms in spring, but it also holds the title of "the latest place in Honshu to welcome autumn leaves". From late November to mid-December each year, the park's impressive collection of over 500 ancient maple trees reaches its peak, offering a rare opportunity to photograph early winter foliage alongside early spring plum blossoms.

The core visual feature is the exquisite interplay of a serene stream, ancient stone bridges, and the mirrored reflections of autumn leaves. The colorful foliage blooms along the gentle gravel paths, and when the calm water perfectly reflects the park's vintage wooden bridges and vibrant autumn colors, the scene becomes rich in layers, exuding an elegant Eastern Zen atmosphere.

The park is well-planned with gentle terrain, making it ideal for families to stroll and savor history together. During the autumn foliage season, a magical illumination event takes place at night, enhancing the ethereal reflections. Visiting on weekday mornings ensures fewer crowds, perfect for leisurely walks and capturing stunning photos.

濱松城.count: numerous colorful leaves throughout the castle park
濱松城.fee: 天守閣大人 200 円（公園免費）
濱松城.address: 靜岡縣浜松市中央区元城町100-2
濱松城.access_car: about 20 minutes from Tomei Expressway Hamamatsu IC
濱松城.tagline: Explore the birthplace of Tokugawa Ieyasu and bask in a fiery sea of autumn leaves.
濱松城.desc:
Hamamatsu Castle, built during the Edo period, gained national fame as the place where Tokugawa Ieyasu spent his youth, affectionately known as "Shusse Castle." From late November to early December each year, the park comes alive with hundreds of maple trees turning vibrant shades of red, painting the landscape in soft autumn hues.

The highlight here is the classic scenery of "wild stone walls, restored keep, and autumn leaves." The colorful foliage blooms alongside the gentle waterways, and when the calm water perfectly reflects the rugged castle walls and the vibrant forest, it creates a spacious and majestic view of this historic site.

The park's terrain is completely flat, with well-maintained ancient pathways, making it very friendly for seniors and strollers. Inside the keep, you'll find a rich display of historical artifacts. Set aside an hour for a leisurely stroll; the early morning hours on weekdays see the fewest crowds, making it the perfect time for capturing the beauty of this historic castle.

名古屋城.count: vibrant autumn leaves throughout the castle site
名古屋城.address: 愛知縣名古屋市中区本丸1-1
名古屋城.access_train: About a 5-minute walk from Nagoya City Subway 'Shiyakusho Station'
名古屋城.access_car: About a 5-minute drive from Nagoya Expressway 'Marunouchi IC'
名古屋城.tagline: Discover Japan's historic gem, where golden rooftops meet vibrant autumn leaves.
名古屋城.desc:
Nagoya Castle, commissioned by Tokugawa Ieyasu, stands as a magnificent historical site among Japan's three great castles. From late November to early December, the hundreds of maple trees surrounding the grand old moat, Honmaru Palace, and the scenic Ni-no-Maru Garden burst into a stunning display of red.

The main highlight is the classic view of the golden shachihoko-topped tenshu, robust stone walls, and the colorful autumn foliage. The vibrant forest embraces ancient moss and towering trees, and when the calm moat perfectly reflects the majestic tenshu and the full palette of autumn colors, the scene opens up to reveal the ultimate in Japanese aesthetic beauty.

The park's terrain is completely flat, with well-maintained stone paths that are very friendly for seniors and strollers. The newly restored interior of Honmaru Palace features exquisite wooden textures, making it a must-visit. Early mornings on weekdays see the fewest crowds, making it the perfect time for capturing the historical castle in all its mirrored glory.

東山動植物園.address: 愛知縣名古屋市千種区東山元町3-70
東山動植物園.access_train: About a 3-minute walk from Higashiyama Park Station on the Nagoya City Subway Higashiyama Line
東山動植物園.access_car: About a 5-minute drive from the Higashiyama Park IC on the Nagoya Expressway
東山動植物園.tagline: A serene urban oasis of nature, stunning with traditional thatched houses, a large pond, and vibrant maple leaf corridors.
東山動植物園.desc:
Higashiyama Zoo and Botanical Gardens is a massive urban nature park that combines a large botanical garden, a zoo, and historical landmarks. Every year from mid to late November, the area surrounding the historic "Gassho-zukuri" farmhouse, relocated from Gifu Prefecture, is adorned with hundreds of maple trees reaching their peak autumn colors.

The core visual highlight is the "traditional thatched roof, tranquil pond, and vibrant autumn leaves" perfectly framed together. The colorful foliage stretches along gentle riverbanks and gravel paths, and when the calm water reflects the rustic old buildings and the vibrant autumn colors, it creates an expansive sense of space, showcasing exquisite horticultural beauty.

The park's pathways are fully paved and accessible, making it perfect for families with elderly members or strollers to stroll leisurely. The famous Higashiyama Sky Tower is also located within the park, offering a panoramic view. Set aside two hours for a leisurely walk, with weekday mornings being the most tranquil, ideal for capturing stunning outdoor shots.

定光寺.count: autumn leaves in the deep mountains surrounding the temple
定光寺.address: 愛知縣瀬戸市定光寺町373
定光寺.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from JR Chuo Main Line "Shidokouji Station"
定光寺.access_car: About a 20-minute drive from Tomei Expressway "Kasugai IC"
定光寺.tagline: Discover the sacred beauty of the Tokugawa family's shrine amidst a vibrant tunnel of autumn leaves.
定光寺.desc:
Shidokouji is an ancient Rinzai Zen temple located at the foot of the mountains in Seto City, boasting over 1,300 years of history. From mid-November to late November, the approach to the main hall, Muei-den, and the nationally important cultural property, Minamoto no Yoshikiyo Shrine, is lined with hundreds of maple trees and maple groves, creating a serene and sacred atmosphere.

The most iconic scene features the "sturdy wooden sanmon gate, stone steps, and the historical dialogue with the maple forest." The colorful foliage embraces the rustic stone lanterns and the towering ancient trees, and when the afternoon mist drifts through the mountains, the vibrant colors piercing through the fog beautifully embody the temple's unique tranquil and historical Zen essence.

Reaching the main hall and shrine requires climbing some natural stone paths and long steps, so it's highly recommended to wear comfortable, non-slip sneakers for your visit. The surrounding environment is peaceful, and the area is relatively quiet for tourists. Early mornings on weekdays are the most ethereal, making it the perfect time for capturing stunning traditional cultural landscapes.

彥根城.count: over 500 trees in the castle park and Genkyu-en
彥根城.access_train: 15-minute walk from JR Biwako Line 'Hikone Station'
彥根城.access_car: about 10 minutes by car from Meishin Expressway 'Hikone IC'
彥根城.tagline: Experience the majestic Hikone Castle and the stunning autumn reflections of Genkyu-en Garden.
彥根城.desc:
Hikone Castle, built during the Keicho era, is a magnificent castle that has preserved its Edo-period structure and is recognized as one of Japan's national treasures. From late November to early December each year, the renowned daimyo garden, Genkyu-en, adjacent to the castle's moat, welcomes the vibrant colors of autumn, adding a splash of seasonal beauty to the castle's grand white walls.

The core highlights include the national treasure, the three-story tenshu, the scenic views of Lake Biwa, and the classical backdrop of autumn leaves. The colorful foliage blooms alongside the gentle waterways, and when the tranquil pond reflects the towering tenshu, the vintage wooden bridges, and the vibrant canopy of leaves, it creates an expansive scene that showcases the ultimate in Japanese aesthetic beauty.

The castle grounds are situated on natural hills, and reaching the tenshu requires climbing a steep set of ancient stone steps, so wearing non-slip athletic shoes is recommended. Meanwhile, the flat terrain of Genkyu-en is easily accessible. During the autumn festival, enchanting night illuminations are held. For the most serene experience, visit early on weekday mornings.

石山寺.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from Ishiyama-dera Station on the Keihan Ishiyama Sakamoto Line
石山寺.access_car: About a 10-minute drive from the Setahigashi IC on the Meishin Expressway
石山寺.tagline: Discover the enchanting realm of Ishiyama-dera, where ancient stones meet vibrant autumn leaves.
石山寺.desc:
Ishiyama-dera is a renowned temple located by the Setogawa River, founded by the monk Ryōben and famous for being the inspiration behind Murasaki Shikibu's "The Tale of Genji." Each year, from mid-November to late November, the temple grounds come alive as hundreds of ancient maple trees burst into color around the impressive natural monument of "Ishiyama" rock formations.

The most iconic view features the striking contrast between the unique black Ishiyama cliffs, the Tahōtō pagoda, and the vibrant maple forest. The rustic wooden main hall stretches across the rugged rocks, with a sea of deep red maple leaves cascading down the valley. When the night illumination begins, a dreamlike play of light dances across the colorful foliage, creating a profound atmosphere.

The temple's layout is well-planned, with flat gravel paths, though some routes to the Tahōtō pagoda feature natural stone steps, so wearing comfortable shoes is recommended. During the maple festival, the temple charges a small entrance fee and offers limited edition goshuin stamps. Early mornings on weekdays provide the most ethereal atmosphere, perfect for photography.

比叡山延曆寺.count: abundant autumn leaves throughout the vast temple grounds
比叡山延曆寺.address: 滋賀縣大津市坂本本町4220
比叡山延曆寺.access_train: Take the JR train to "Hieizan Sakamoto Station" and ride the cable car for about 11 minutes to "Enryakuji Station"
比叡山延曆寺.access_car: Approximately 40 minutes from Meishin Expressway "Kyoto Higashi IC"
比叡山延曆寺.tagline: Explore the sacred forest of Hieizan, where vibrant autumn colors meet ancient temples.
比叡山延曆寺.desc:
Hieizan Enryakuji is a UNESCO World Heritage site straddling Kyoto and Shiga, revered as the mother of Japanese Buddhism. At an elevation of 800 meters, the autumn leaves here bloom earlier than elsewhere, transforming the sacred mountain into a breathtaking tapestry of rich reds and yellows from late October to mid-November.

The highlight is the dialogue between the vividly colored foliage and the historical ambiance of the ancient structures along the Hieizan Scenic Road. The vibrant colors stretch along the ancient stone-paved paths, particularly in the "Tohto" area, where ancient buildings stand against a backdrop of colorful cliffs, creating an ethereal scene when the afternoon mist rolls in.

Given the vastness of the area (divided into Tohto, Saikyo, and Yokokawa), it is highly recommended to drive slowly or take the convenient shuttle bus. The mountain area can be quite chilly and breezy in the mornings and evenings, so be sure to bring a warm jacket for your visit. For the best experience, visit on weekday mornings to enjoy the unique tranquility of the mountains.

嵐山.fee: 免費（天龍寺庭園 500 円）
嵐山.address: 京都府京都市右京区嵯峨天龍寺芒ノ馬場町
嵐山.access_train: A short walk from Hankyu Arashiyama Line 'Arashiyama Station'; about a 5-minute walk from JR Saga-Arashiyama Line 'Saga-Arashiyama Station'
嵐山.access_car: Approximately a 5-minute drive from Kyoto Jukan Expressway 'Arashiyama IC'
嵐山.desc:
Arashiyama is a vast resort that beautifully blends historical landmarks, bamboo groves, and nature. From late November to early December, the area bursts into a riot of colors during the peak of autumn foliage. The maple trees and sweetgum forests transform the mountainsides into a stunning display of red and gold, creating a vivid autumn atmosphere from the banks of the Togetsukyo Bridge to Tenryuji Temple and along the Hozu Gorge.

The main highlight is the classic scene of the Togetsukyo Bridge, traditional yakkōbune boats, and the vibrant colored forests. Strolling along the gentle paths by the Ōi River, you can see traditional wooden boats gliding through the emerald waters, set against the backdrop of the fiery red and golden mountains of Arashiyama, creating a striking visual experience.

The area is extensive, so it's highly recommended to combine your visit with a ride on the Arashiyama Scenic Train or a sightseeing boat along the Hozu River to enjoy the autumn colors from different perspectives. With plenty of shade and a cool breeze, this destination is perfect for a day trip. Early mornings on weekdays see the fewest crowds, making it ideal for leisurely walks and scenic photography.
嵐山.tagline: Experience the vibrant tapestry of autumn at Arashiyama's breathtaking landscapes.

東福寺.count: approximately 2,000 maple trees under the Tōkyō Bridge
東福寺.fee: 大人 600 円（通天橋・開山堂）
東福寺.address: 京都府京都市東山区本町15丁目778
東福寺.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from JR Nara Line and Keihan Main Line "Tofuku-ji Station"
東福寺.access_car: About a 10-minute drive from Hanshin Expressway "Kamogawa Higashi IC"
東福寺.desc:
Tofuku-ji is a premier Zen temple located in Kyoto's Higashiyama district, renowned as one of the Five Mountains of Kyoto. Each year, from late November to early December, around 2,000 "Tōkyō maple" trees surrounding the steep Washigamine Valley transform in unison, painting the entire mountainous gorge in vibrant hues of red and gold.

The most stunning highlight here is the view from the "Tōkyō Bridge" overlooking the fiery autumn leaves of Washigamine. Standing on the expansive wooden corridor bridge, visitors can gaze down at the sea of brilliant red maples below, resembling crashing waves. As the morning light filters through the trees, the scene becomes a striking visual feast.

As one of Kyoto's top spots for autumn foliage, the Tōkyō Bridge and Wagyū Bridge prohibit stopping for photography during peak season to ensure safety. The temple grounds feature natural gravel paths and ancient stone steps, requiring a bit of stamina to navigate. It’s best to arrive right when the gates open on weekdays to fully enjoy the serene atmosphere and perfect lighting for photos.
東福寺.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of autumn leaves at Tofuku-ji's iconic bridge.

南禪寺.count: numerous autumn leaves around the temple and aqueduct
南禪寺.fee: 境內免費（方丈庭園 600 円）
南禪寺.address: 京都府京都市左京区南禪寺福地町
南禪寺.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from Keage Station on the Tozai Line
南禪寺.access_car: Approximately a 20-minute drive from Meishin Expressway, Kyoto Higashi IC
南禪寺.tagline: Experience the zen beauty of Nanzen-ji, where ancient gardens meet vibrant autumn leaves.
南禪寺.desc:
Nanzen-ji is ranked among the top Zen temples in Japan's "Kyoto Gozan." From late November to early December, the expansive ancient courtyard and the area around the Sanmon gate come alive with hundreds of maple trees at their peak. The grand wooden structures are enveloped in brilliant red foliage, transforming this site into a renowned autumn destination in Kyoto.

The visual highlight is the "National Important Cultural Property Sanmon, a classic dialogue between moss and the red-brick aqueduct." The retro arched drainage channel built during the Meiji era frames the cascading hues of red and yellow leaves above, along with the rustic stone steps, creating a harmonious scene that perfectly embodies the historical essence of this ancient temple.

The park's terrain is completely flat, and the stone pathways are well-maintained, making it very friendly for elderly visitors and those with strollers. Nearby, you can explore the famous Hojo Garden and Tenju-an, where you can also savor traditional yudofu. Visiting on weekday mornings ensures fewer crowds, making it ideal for capturing the timeless beauty of this cultural gem.

北野天滿宮.count: approximately 350 maple trees on the premises
北野天滿宮.address: 京都府京都市上京区馬喰町
北野天滿宮.access_train: A short walk from the city bus stop "Kitano Tenmangu Mae"
北野天滿宮.access_car: About a 30-minute drive from Meishin Expressway "Kyoto Minami IC"
北野天滿宮.tagline: Discover the serene beauty of Kyoto's iconic autumn leaves at Kitano Tenmangu Shrine.
北野天滿宮.desc:
Kitano Tenmangu is the head shrine of all Tenmangu shrines across Japan. From late November to early December, the western side of the shrine, featuring the historical earthen ramparts "Odoi" built by Toyotomi Hideyoshi, and the adjacent "Momiji-en" garden, open to the public for a limited time. Here, around 350 ancient maple trees transform the gentle riverside into a stunning spectacle of color.

The most iconic view combines the "Vermilion Ume Bridge, the flowing Kawai River, and the vibrant autumn leaves". The colorful foliage flanks the tranquil stream, and when the calm water perfectly reflects the historic bridge, ancient stone lanterns, and the vibrant canopy, it creates an expansive scene that showcases the elegance of traditional Japanese aesthetics.

The paths within the Momiji-en are designed to follow the natural terrain, making for a pleasant and comfortable stroll. During the flower festival, enchanting illumination events enhance the ethereal reflections. Early morning visits on weekdays are the best time to avoid crowds, perfect for capturing the beauty of this historic site.

貴船神社.count: approximately 200 maple trees along the approach
貴船神社.address: 京都府京都市左京区鞍馬貴船町180
貴船神社.access_train: About a 30-minute walk from Eizan Electric Railway "Kifune-guchi Station" or take a bus to "Kifune"
貴船神社.access_car: About a 40-minute drive from Meishin Expressway "Kyoto Higashi IC"
貴船神社.tagline: Step into the serene depths of the mountains and experience the beauty of spring lanterns and ancient cedar forests.
貴船神社.desc:
Kifune Jinja, nestled in the deep valleys at the foot of Kurama Mountain, is an ancient shrine that protects water sources and boasts a rich history. Due to its high-altitude climate, the blooming period here is later than in the plains, with vibrant Iroha maple trees reaching their peak from early to mid-November, adorning the sacred grounds with autumn colors.

The most representative aspect of its Japanese aesthetic is the dialogue between the "long spring lantern stone steps, the vividly colored old shrine, and the historical maple forest." The colorful foliage embraces the neatly lined vermilion lanterns on both sides, and as dusk falls and the lanterns light up, the interplay of light and shadow on the foliage reveals the ultimate expression of Eastern religious beauty and mysterious Zen spirit.

To reach the main shrine, visitors must climb hundreds of ancient stone steps, so wearing comfortable, non-slip athletic shoes is highly recommended for those coming to enjoy the autumn leaves. Morning fog often blankets the mountains, creating a mystical beauty among the stone steps and colorful foliage. Riding the Eizan Railway through the "Maple Tunnel" is also a must-do for seasoned leaf peepers.

大阪城公園.count: numerous colorful leaves throughout the park
大阪城公園.fee: 公園免費（天守閣大人 600 円）
大阪城公園.address: 大阪府大阪市中央区大阪城1-1
大阪城公園.access_train: A short walk from JR Osaka Loop Line "Osaka Castle Park Station"
大阪城公園.access_car: About a 5-minute drive from Hanshin Expressway "Morinomiya IC"
大阪城公園.tagline: Capture the essence of history amidst vibrant autumn leaves and majestic landscapes.
大阪城公園.desc:
Osaka Castle Park is a sprawling historical urban park that surrounds the magnificent old moat and the Honmaru Palace. Every year from early November to early December, the park's famous maple-lined paths reach their peak, as hundreds of ancient ginkgo trees turn a brilliant red, adding a splash of color to the imposing castle walls in early autumn.

The park's core feature is the "sturdy stone walls, white-walled golden castle, and the classical backdrop of autumn leaves." The colorful foliage blooms alongside the gentle waterways, and when the calm moat perfectly reflects the towering castle, the ancient ginkgo trees, some over three hundred years old, and the vibrant canopy, the scene opens up to reveal the grandeur of this historic city.

The park's terrain is completely flat, with well-maintained ancient pathways that are very friendly for seniors and strollers. Surrounding it are the lush Nishinomaru Garden and historical exhibition halls, making it ideal for leisurely travelers. Early mornings on weekdays see the fewest crowds, perfect for capturing the stunning reflections of this historic landmark.

萬博紀念公園.count: approximately 3,800 colorful maple trees in the park
萬博紀念公園.fee: 大人 260 円（自然文化園）
萬博紀念公園.address: 大阪府吹田市千里万博公園1-1
萬博紀念公園.access_train: A short walk from Osaka Monorail 'Banpaku Kinen Koen Station'
萬博紀念公園.access_car: About a 10-minute drive from Meishin Expressway 'Suita IC'
萬博紀念公園.tagline: Experience the modern beauty of the 'Tower of the Sun' and Momiji River's autumn splendor at the former World Expo site.
萬博紀念公園.desc:
Banpaku Kinen Koen is a vast urban oasis transformed from the site of the 1970 World Expo.
