Every year from early to late November, the 'Momiji River' located to the west of the Japanese Garden and the surrounding Natural Cultural Park come alive with over 20 varieties of maple trees, showcasing around 10,000 vibrant maple and zelkova trees in their peak glory, painting the expansive park in a kaleidoscope of colors.

The visual centerpiece is the harmonious interplay of the avant-garde Tower of the Sun, the clear streams, and the vibrant floral carpets. Stroll along the well-maintained, gently winding gravel paths, where ribbons of red and yellow flowers stretch alongside the serene river. As the morning sunlight filters through the treetops, casting a warm glow on the flowing waters of the Momiji River waterfall, it frames the majestic Tower of the Sun in the distance, revealing a refined modern landscape aesthetic.

The park's terrain is mostly completely flat, with well-constructed, accessible pathways and wooden walkways that are very friendly for seniors and strollers. During the Momiji Festival, the tea house in the Japanese Garden opens exclusively for visitors. The park opens at 9:30 AM on weekdays, making it the perfect spot for capturing the pure, breathtaking autumn foliage.

勝尾寺.address: 大阪府箕面市粟生間谷2914-1
勝尾寺.access_train: About 30 minutes by bus from Kitakinki-Toyooka Line "Senri Chuo Station"
勝尾寺.access_car: Approximately 30 minutes by car from the Kinki Expressway "Toyonaka Minami IC"
勝尾寺.desc:
Katsuouji, nestled in the mountains of Minoh City, is a millennium-old temple renowned for its prayers for good fortune and its famous "Katsuun Daruma" dolls. From late November to early December, the expansive grounds, built into the mountainside, come alive with thousands of maple and beech trees transforming into a stunning tapestry of autumn colors.

The highlight here is the enchanting "misty lakeside forest in dialogue with the history of Daruma dolls." Blue and purple floral clusters spread along the banks of Kannon Pond and the hillside. As the pond releases ethereal white mist at intervals, it creates a magical scene alongside countless red Daruma dolls hidden among the flowers, especially captivating when illuminated at night.

Since the temple is built on a hillside, the approach includes many steps, so wearing comfortable, non-slip shoes is highly recommended. The area has limited shade, so bringing a sun umbrella is advisable. Visiting on weekday mornings offers a quieter experience, making it the perfect time to capture the serene beauty of the sacred grounds and the grand Daruma display.
勝尾寺.tagline: Discover the sacred grounds of Katsuouji, where fortune meets the vibrant hues of autumn.

姬路城.count: abundant colorful leaves around the castle park and Kokoen Garden
姬路城.access_car: approximately 20 minutes from Sanyo Expressway "Sanyo Himeji East IC"
姬路城.tagline: Experience the majestic Himeji Castle, where vibrant autumn leaves dance around a historic masterpiece.
姬路城.desc:
Himeji Castle is Japan's first UNESCO World Heritage site and one of the twelve remaining original castles. From late October to mid-November, the famous "Sannomaru Plaza" and the old moat transform into a sea of red with hundreds of maple trees, beautifully contrasting against the castle's pure white walls.

The core feature is the "pure white main keep, sturdy stone walls, and the classical backdrop of autumn leaves." The colorful foliage blooms alongside the gentle waterways, and when the calm moat perfectly reflects the magnificent national treasure and the vibrant colors, the scene feels expansive and harmonious, showcasing the grandeur of this historic city.

The castle's terrain is set on natural hills, and reaching the main keep requires climbing a steep set of ancient stone steps, so wearing non-slip athletic shoes is recommended. The interior of the keep is well-preserved. Allocate about 1.5 hours for your visit, and early mornings on weekdays are the best times to avoid crowds and capture stunning photos of this historical gem.

六甲山.count: Abundant autumn leaves near the mountaintop and around Arima Onsen
六甲山.address: 兵庫縣神戸市灘区六甲山町北六甲4512-98
六甲山.access_train: About 30 minutes by bus from Hankyu Kobe Line "Rokko Station"
六甲山.access_car: About 20 minutes by car from Hanshin Expressway "Nishinomiya Yamate IC"
六甲山.tagline: Ride the vintage Rokko cable car for breathtaking views of the Seto Inland Sea and vibrant mountain flower displays.
六甲山.desc:
Rokko Mountain is a stunning year-round resort located near Kobe, where every season brings its own beauty. From mid-October to mid-November, the once lush green slopes transform into a vibrant tapestry of autumn colors, as thousands of maple and beech trees change their hues, painting the entire landscape in brilliant shades.

The ultimate visual experience here is the "panoramic view from the high observation deck, showcasing the magnificent colorful forest." Visitors can take the "Rokko Cable Car" to ascend slowly, feeling the crisp highland breeze as they gaze down from a bird's-eye view at the endless valleys adorned with a colorful carpet of yellow and red, all while taking in the vastness of the Seto Inland Sea.

Due to its high altitude, the temperature here is cooler than at sea level, making it an ideal spot for early autumn leaf viewing. The open highland terrain offers little shelter, so be sure to secure your tripod and wear a hat while taking photos. Climbing on a clear morning provides the best visibility, allowing you to capture the expansive fields and stunning floral scenery perfectly.

有馬溫泉.count: Vibrant autumn leaves around the hot spring street and Ryohoji Park
有馬溫泉.address: 兵庫縣神戸市北区有馬町
有馬溫泉.access_train: A short walk from Kobe Electric Railway "Arima Onsen Station"
有馬溫泉.access_car: About a 20-minute drive from Hanshin Expressway "Nishinomiya Yamate IC"
有馬溫泉.tagline: Discover Japan's ancient hot spring valley, where vibrant autumn leaves and serene temples await.
有馬溫泉.desc:
Arima Onsen, alongside Dogo and Shirahama, is one of Japan's three ancient hot spring towns with a rich history. Every year from early to mid-November, the nationally designated scenic spot, Ryohoji Park, comes alive with over two thousand ancient maple trees bursting into color, transforming the lush green valley into a vibrant tapestry of reds and yellows.

The most iconic view features the "Hōjō Hideyoshi's Evening Garden, the vermilion Nene Bridge, and the dialogue of autumn leaves." The colorful foliage surrounds ancient stone lanterns, and when the lights illuminate at night, the entire mountain transforms into a golden world, perfectly contrasting with the tranquil water surface, embodying Eastern religious aesthetics.

The hot spring street and park trails are well-maintained, making it ideal for a leisurely morning stroll and a day of relaxation in the onsen. As a world-class attraction, it often attracts large crowds during the day. We highly recommend visiting on weekday mornings or during the evening when the lights are on, as this is when you can capture the most story-filled cultural scenery.

奈良公園.count: vast areas of colorful leaves throughout the park
奈良公園.address: 奈良縣奈良市登大路町30
奈良公園.access_train: About a 5-minute walk from Kintetsu Nara Station on the Kintetsu Nara Line
奈良公園.access_car: Approximately a 20-minute drive from Tenri IC on the Nishi-Meihan Expressway
奈良公園.tagline: Discover the ancient oasis of Nara Park, where wild deer and golden ginkgo leaves create a breathtaking autumn tapestry.
奈良公園.desc:
Nara Park is a vast urban oasis spanning 500 hectares, encompassing world heritage sites like Todai-ji and Kasuga-taisha. From early November to early December, the park's famous maple fields reach their peak, with hundreds of towering ginkgo and maple trees transforming into vibrant hues, painting the landscape with early autumn colors.

The most iconic scene here is the "dialogue between wild deer and the golden carpet of fallen leaves." Amidst ancient stone lanterns and the flowing waters of the Yoshikigawa, herds of deer stroll across the leaf-covered golden blanket, framed by the colorful foliage above, showcasing nature's perfect religious aesthetics.

The park's terrain is completely flat, with well-maintained walking paths that are very friendly for seniors and strollers. The surrounding facilities are excellent, making it ideal for a day trip. Visiting early in the morning on weekdays offers the least crowds, as sunlight filters through the treetops, casting beautiful reflections on the colorful foliage, perfect for photography.

吉野山.count: The mountain is filled with vibrant autumn leaves, a breathtaking sight alongside spring's Yoshinoyama.
吉野山.fee: 免費（吉野山ロープウェイ大人 450 円）
吉野山.address: 奈良縣吉野郡吉野町吉野山
吉野山.access_train: Take the Kintetsu Yoshino Line to Yoshino Station, then ride the cable car or walk.
吉野山.access_car: Approximately 50 minutes drive from the Nishimeihan Expressway, Koriyama IC.
吉野山.tagline: Experience a breathtaking tapestry of 30,000 vibrant cherry and maple trees on this sacred mountain.
吉野山.desc:
Yoshinoyama is a UNESCO World Heritage site renowned for its stunning "Ippon Senbon" cherry blossoms. Every year, from mid-October to early November, the 30,000 trees on the mountain transform into a vibrant canvas of red and gold, creating a spectacular natural carpet across the highlands.  
  
The most striking view can be found at the Kimpusen-ji Zao-do, where the deep valleys and colorful forests create a stunning three-dimensional composition. From Yoshimizu Shrine or Hanayagura Observation Deck, the gradient of red and yellow stretches along the steep slopes, beautifully framed by ancient wooden temples, showcasing a grand religious aesthetic.  
  
While you can take the Yoshinoyama Ropeway to ascend the mountain, the attractions at the summit are spread out, with natural slopes connecting the shrines. Be sure to wear professional non-slip hiking shoes. The mountain often experiences morning fog, and the colorful forests shrouded in mist offer a mysterious and ethereal beauty. For the best photos, choose a clear morning with good visibility.

長谷寺.count: over 1,000 trees along both sides of the corridor and within the grounds
長谷寺.access_train: Approximately a 15-minute walk from Kintetsu Osaka Line 'Hase-dera Station'
長谷寺.access_car: About a 30-minute drive from the Nishi-Meihan Expressway 'Tenri IC'
長谷寺.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of autumn at the sacred 'Temple of Flowers' beneath the slopes of Hase Mountain.
長谷寺.desc:
Hase-dera, nestled at the foot of Hase Mountain, is an ancient temple belonging to the Shingon sect of the Buzan school, renowned throughout Japan as the 'Temple of Flowers.' From mid-November to early December, hundreds of vibrant Iroha maple trees burst into color around the main hall, painting the sacred grounds in rich autumn hues.

The most iconic view features the 'Important Cultural Property: the 362-step long corridor and its classical dialogue with the autumn leaves.' The rustic wooden corridor stretches along the mountainside, with light filtering through the lattice windows showcasing a tapestry of red and yellow maple forests. Ascend to the suspended 'Hall Stage' of the main hall for a breathtaking panorama of the entire valley adorned in fall colors.

The stone steps of the long corridor are well-maintained and covered for rain protection, but the temple is built on a hillside, requiring a bit of stamina to navigate. Comfortable walking shoes are recommended. Early mornings on weekdays offer the most ethereal atmosphere, especially during the morning prayers, making it the perfect time for capturing the temple's ancient charm in photographs.

室生寺.count: abundant autumn leaves around the five-story pagoda and temple grounds
室生寺.access_train: About 15 minutes by bus from Kintetsu Osaka Line 'Muromiguchi Ohno Station'
室生寺.access_car: Approximately 60 minutes by car from the Nishi-Meihan Expressway 'Tenri IC'
室生寺.tagline: Discover the sacred beauty of Muromiji Temple, where women can pilgrimage to the National Treasure five-story pagoda and the vibrant maple tunnel.
室生寺.desc:
Muromiji Temple is nestled deep within the mountains, renowned as the 'Women's Koyasan' for allowing female worshippers since ancient times. From mid-November to early December each year, hundreds of ancient maple trees transform, adorning the sacred grounds and surrounding halls with a classical elegance and profound atmosphere.

The most iconic view features the 'National Treasure Small Wooden Five-Story Pagoda,' the rustic moss-covered stone steps, and the vibrant dialogue of autumn leaves. Particularly striking is the view from the Nio-mon gate, where the natural stone steps of Yoroi-zaka are enveloped in a cascade of brilliant red maple shadows, embracing the layers of ancient wooden halls that showcase the temple's unique historical and mysterious Zen essence.

The main pilgrimage route to the Taiko-do and five-story pagoda primarily consists of natural stone steps, with some sections featuring slight elevation changes. For a comfortable experience and great photo opportunities, wearing professional, comfortable running shoes is highly recommended. During the maple festival, enchanting night illuminations are held. On regular days, entering at dawn offers the most ethereal atmosphere, perfect for capturing the unique cultural landscape of this Zen temple.

那智大瀑布.count: abundant red leaves around the waterfall's pristine forest
那智大瀑布.fee: 免費（飛瀧神社御瀧拝観料 300 円）
那智大瀑布.address: 和歌山縣東牟婁郡那智勝浦町那智山
那智大瀑布.access_train: Take a bus from JR Kisei Line 'Kiikatsuura Station' for about 25 minutes to 'Nachiyama'
那智大瀑布.access_car: Approximately 80 minutes drive from Hanwa Expressway 'Nanki Tanabe IC'
那智大瀑布.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of Nachi Falls and vibrant autumn leaves in a UNESCO World Heritage site.
那智大瀑布.desc:
Nachi Falls is nestled within the sacred grounds of Kumano Nachi Taisha and Seiganto-ji, plunging an impressive 133 meters. Revered as a divine entity of Nachiyama faith, it is recognized as a UNESCO World Heritage site. From late November to early December, the ancient broadleaf forests surrounding the steep cliffs and deep valleys transform into a vibrant tapestry of colors, enhancing this natural wonder.

The most stunning view features a dramatic scene of "triple cliffs, cascading waterfalls, three-story pagoda, and autumn leaves." Standing on the classic viewing platform, you'll witness the turquoise waters rushing down in a vertical cascade, with mist rising from the rocks below. To your right, the vermilion three-story pagoda of Seiganto-ji contrasts beautifully with the colorful carpet of yellow and red leaves, creating an expansive and majestic atmosphere.

The path to the base of the falls, leading to the Hirou Shrine, is equipped with well-maintained, gentle stone steps, making it relatively easy and safe to navigate. However, due to the abundant mist from the falls, some sections may be slippery, so wearing good non-slip athletic shoes is highly recommended for leaf-peeping and photography. For the best experience, visit on a clear morning when the sunlight perfectly illuminates the stunning scenery of water and red maple leaves.

和歌山城.count: abundant colorful leaves throughout the park
和歌山城.fee: 天守閣大人 410 円（公園免費）
和歌山城.address: 和歌山縣和歌山市一番丁3
和歌山城.access_train: About 10 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Line 'Wakayama Station'
和歌山城.access_car: Approximately 10 minutes by car from Hanwa Expressway 'Wakayama IC'
和歌山城.tagline: Experience the majestic beauty of Wakayama Castle and its stunning gardens in a vibrant autumn setting.
和歌山城.desc:
Wakayama Castle, perched atop the historic Torafusu Mountain, is one of Japan's top 100 castles and a renowned stronghold of the Kishu Tokugawa clan. From late November to early December, the famous Nishinomaru Garden within the castle grounds bursts into a spectacular display of autumn leaves, exuding classical elegance.

The main highlight is the interplay of the 'white castle tower, sukiya-style Koi Pavilion, and the vibrant autumn foliage.' The colorful trees surround the clear inner moat, and when the calm, mirror-like water perfectly reflects the historic wooden structures and the vivid foliage, it creates an expansive scene that showcases the beauty of traditional Japanese gardens.

The castle's terrain is naturally hilly, and reaching the castle tower requires climbing a steep set of ancient stone steps, so wearing non-slip athletic shoes is recommended. Within the garden, the Ochaya Shōmatsu-an tea house offers a chance to enjoy matcha while admiring the maple leaves. Arriving early on weekdays allows you to fully immerse yourself in the tranquil atmosphere and capture stunning photos in this historic garden.

根來寺.count: approximately 100 maple trees and numerous colorful leaves
根來寺.access_train: 15 minutes by taxi from JR "Iwadeyama Station"
根來寺.access_car: About 5 minutes by car from Hanwa Expressway "Iwadekonai IC"
根來寺.tagline: Discover the serene beauty of Japan's largest wooden pagoda amidst vibrant autumn leaves.
根來寺.desc:
根来寺, founded by the revered monk Kyōban, is a historic temple known for its powerful warrior monks, the 根来众, during the Sengoku period. Each year, from late November to early December, the temple welcomes visitors to its stunning autumn foliage season. The original forests lining the 大門川 stream in the "紅葉谷 (Momiji Valley)" transform into a vibrant tapestry of red, adorning this sacred site with life and color.

The main visual attraction is the "National Treasure, Japan's tallest wooden pagoda," which stands majestically at 40 meters. Its pure white structure beautifully reflects in the tranquil waters, surrounded by blooming colorful flowers, creating a harmonious blend of stillness and historical Zen ambiance.

The walking paths between the main halls are flat and spacious, making it very friendly for elderly visitors, allowing for a leisurely morning stroll without any burden. The expansive grounds of 根来寺, along with the nearby 根来SL Park, are perfect for a day trip. Visiting in the early morning offers the best chance to capture the ethereal Zen atmosphere and the perfect backdrop of this historic site without the crowds.

大山.count: Vibrant autumn leaves around Daisen-ji and Gōen-zan
大山.address: 鳥取縣西伯郡大山町大山
大山.access_train: Take a bus from JR San'in Main Line 'Yonago Station' for about 50 minutes to 'Daisen-ji'
大山.access_car: Approximately a 20-minute drive from the Yonago Expressway 'Mizuguchi IC'
大山.tagline: Experience the breathtaking golden hues of 'Hōki Fuji' amidst Japan's largest beech forest.
大山.desc:
Daisen, known as 'Hōki Fuji' for its resemblance to Mount Fuji, is the highest peak in the Chūgoku region. From late October to early November, the vast beech forest at the foot of the mountain transforms into a vibrant tapestry of colors, painting the majestic and rugged mountain range in hues of purple and red.  

The most stunning visual highlight is the view from Jikengatake Observatory, where the stark white cliffs contrast beautifully with the colorful forest below, creating a breathtaking scene that showcases the grandeur of nature.  

Access to the main viewpoints is easy via well-maintained scenic roads, making it convenient to drive. As this area is at a high altitude, early mornings in late autumn can be quite chilly, so be sure to bring warm, windproof clothing for your visit. For the best experience, aim to visit during a clear morning when the mountain's contours and the details of the colorful forest are most vivid.

三瓶山.count: Large areas of red leaves from the foot to the mid-slope of the mountain
三瓶山.address: 島根縣大田市三瓶町多根
三瓶山.access_train: About 45 minutes by bus from JR San'in Line "Oda City Station"
三瓶山.access_car: Approximately 30 minutes by car from Hamada Expressway "Mizuho IC"
三瓶山.tagline: Experience the breathtaking interplay of silver grass and vibrant volcanoes at Sanbe-yama!
三瓶山.desc:
Sanbe-yama is a renowned mountain formed by the circular volcanic groups of Male Sanbe and Female Sanbe, located within Daisen-Oki National Park. From mid-October to early November each year, the broadleaf natural forests covering the mountain slopes transform into a rich tapestry of early autumn colors.

The stunning scenery here features the "silver waves of miscanthus grass rolling across the vast Nishinahara grassland, framed by colorful volcanic peaks." The dense fields of miscanthus sway in the cool highland breeze, shimmering with brilliant golden hues, while the backdrop of vibrant volcanic mountains creates a striking visual contrast and a sense of expansive space.

The area around the mountain base offers well-maintained, flat walking paths and scenic chairlifts, making it very friendly for families and seniors. Although the mountain has limited shade, the late autumn weather is extremely comfortable. If you visit early in the morning on a weekday, you might catch a glimpse of the ethereal morning mist, creating a dreamlike atmosphere.

後楽園.count: approximately 200 maple trees in the garden
後楽園.address: 岡山縣岡山市北区後楽園1-5
後楽園.access_train: Take a bus from JR Okayama Station for about 15 minutes to "Kōraku-en Mae"
後楽園.access_car: About a 15-minute drive from Sanyo Expressway "Okayama IC"
後楽園.tagline: Discover Japan's serene beauty in one of its top three gardens, where history meets nature beneath the majestic Okayama Castle.
後楽園.desc:
Kōraku-en is a stunning Edo-period garden meticulously crafted by Okayama's feudal lord, Ikeda Tsunamasa. Recognized as one of Japan's top three gardens, it offers a breathtaking experience. From late November to early December, the "Chiin no Mori" area, home to around a hundred maple trees, bursts into vibrant autumn colors, enhancing the garden's classical elegance.

The visual highlight is the view from "Ishinsan," overlooking Chiin no Mori, the crystal-clear pond, and the striking Okayama Castle. The colorful foliage lines the gentle waterways, and when the tranquil "Sawa no Ike" pond perfectly reflects the meandering stream, thatched tea house, and the dark walls of the "U-jo" castle tower, it showcases the refined aesthetics of Japanese beauty.

The garden's layout is mostly flat, with well-maintained stone paths and grassy areas, making it very accessible for wheelchairs and strollers. During the special evening illumination of the autumn festival, the pond's reflective beauty takes on an ethereal quality. Arriving early on a weekday allows you to capture the serene grandeur of this classical garden.

錦帯橋.count: numerous autumn leaves along the Nishikigawa and Kikko Park
錦帯橋.access_train: Take a bus from JR Sanyo Main Line "Iwakuni Station" for about 15 minutes to "Nishikikyou"
錦帯橋.access_car: Approximately 15 minutes by car from Sanyo Expressway "Iwakuni IC"
錦帯橋.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of Japan's iconic wooden bridge amidst vibrant autumn colors.
錦帯橋.desc:
Nishikikyou is a world-class, unique wooden five-arch bridge that spans the clear waters of the Iwakuni River, recognized as one of Japan's three great bridges. Every year from mid to late November, the nearby Kikko Park and the opposite Momijidani Park come alive with hundreds of ancient maple trees, showcasing their stunning autumn foliage and adding vibrant colors to this historic bridge.

The main attraction lies in the "unique five-arch wooden bridge, framed by the classical scenery of the mountains and autumn leaves." The colorful foliage blooms along the gentle riverbank, and when the calm waters of the Iwakuni River perfectly reflect the elegant lines of the bridge and the vibrant colors of the trees, you can also catch a glimpse of Iwakuni Castle atop the mountain, embodying the ultimate in Japanese aesthetic beauty.

The parks on both sides of the river feature completely flat terrain, and the ancient pathways are well-maintained, making it very friendly for seniors and strollers. The bridge itself has a special wooden stair structure, so be mindful of your steps while walking across. Early mornings on weekdays see the fewest crowds, making it the best time to capture the perfect mirror-like view without anyone around.

内子の町並み.count: autumn leaves along Edo period streets and surrounding mountains
内子の町並み.address: 愛媛縣喜多郡内子町内子
内子の町並み.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from JR Yosan Line 'Uchiko Station'
内子の町並み.access_car: About a 5-minute drive from Matsuyama Expressway 'Uchiko Igozaki IC'
内子の町並み.tagline: Explore the timeless charm of Uchiko's historic streets and vibrant autumn leaves.
内子の町並み.desc:
Uchiko Town is a beautifully preserved area showcasing traditional buildings from the late Edo to Taisho periods, thriving on the production of wax and washi paper. Every year from mid to late November, the ancient trees lining the old streets and their gardens turn a brilliant red, adding a splash of early autumn color to the yellow and white walls.

The main highlight is the classic scenery created by the grand white lacquered merchant houses, gabled wooden architecture, and the stunning autumn foliage. Stroll along the 600-meter-long cobblestone street, where the traditional wooden Uchikoza theater and the deep red maple leaves above create a picturesque scene that transports you back in time, rich with cultural history.

The entire historic street is flat and easy to navigate, making it very friendly for seniors and travelers who enjoy a leisurely pace in a tranquil environment. The crisp autumn mornings are refreshing and comfortable. Visiting on weekday mornings offers the least crowds, perfect for capturing the pure essence of this historic landscape.

四万十川.count: abundant native forest foliage along the river
四万十川.address: 高知縣四万十市西土佐
四万十川.access_train: Transfer from JR Dosan Line 'Kubokawa Station' to the Tosa Line to 'Egawazaki Station'
四万十川.access_car: Approximately 60 minutes from Kochi Expressway 'Shimancho Chuo IC'
四万十川.tagline: Explore Japan's last clear stream, a 200-kilometer natural wonder filled with vibrant autumn colors.
四万十川.desc:
Shimanto River stretches an impressive 196 kilometers and is renowned for being Japan's last clear stream, untouched by large dams. Each year, from mid to late November, the riverbanks adorned with expansive fields and natural forests transform into a stunning tapestry of gold and deep red foliage.

The most iconic sight here is the historic, railing-free 'Sada Chinkabashi' bridge, perfectly framed by the gentle green waters and vibrant autumn colors. Standing by the crystal-clear riverbank, you can admire the bridge without guardrails, set against a backdrop of mountains blanketed in a colorful carpet of yellow and red, creating an expansive sense of space.

The roads along the river are flat and open, equipped with bike rentals and sightseeing boat experiences, making it incredibly family-friendly for a day trip with elders and children alike. With no obstructions, the clear views on sunny mornings showcase the river's transparency, perfect for capturing breathtaking natural scenery that will wow your social media followers.

雲仙仁田峠.count: Vast areas of colorful leaves around the pass create a spectacular view
雲仙仁田峠.address: 長崎縣雲仙市小浜町雲仙
雲仙仁田峠.access_train: Take a bus from Shimabara Station on the Shimabara Railway for about 60 minutes to Nita-toge
雲仙仁田峠.access_car: About 80 minutes drive from Nagasaki Expressway via Isahaya IC
雲仙仁田峠.tagline: Ride the Unzen Ropeway to the sky and be amazed by the stunning views of Mount Fugendake and vibrant autumn leaves.
雲仙仁田峠.desc:
Unzen Nita-toge is located within Unzen-Amakusa National Park, soaring to an elevation of 1,000 meters, making it the premier spot for autumn foliage in Nagasaki. Due to its high altitude and latitude, the leaves here turn earlier than anywhere else in Kyushu, with over a hundred varieties of broadleaf trees in the natural forests of Mount Fugendake reaching their peak from late October to early November.

The breathtaking views are best experienced on the Unzen Ropeway. Visitors can ascend slowly, feeling the refreshing highland breeze while taking in a bird's-eye view of the colorful tapestry of yellow and red leaves stretching infinitely across the valleys. When the calm water of Shimabara Bay and the distant Aso mountain range frame the scene, the grandeur is overwhelming and visually striking.

While the ropeway takes you directly to the observation deck at Myoken-dake, the high-altitude terrain can be windy and cool, so it's essential to bring a warm jacket for outdoor photography. The trails at the summit are well-maintained and friendly for seniors. For the best experience, choose a clear morning with good visibility to capture the expansive fields and vibrant flowers perfectly.

鍋ヶ滝.count: Cave behind the waterfall and surrounding primeval forest
鍋ヶ滝.address: 熊本縣阿蘇郡小国町黒渕
鍋ヶ滝.access_train: About 30 minutes by taxi from JR Kyudai Main Line "Bungo Mori Station"
鍋ヶ滝.access_car: About 30 minutes from "Kusu IC" on the Oita Expressway
鍋ヶ滝.tagline: Discover the mystical Nabegataki Falls, where water curtains and vibrant autumn foliage create a breathtaking spectacle.
鍋ヶ滝.desc:
Nabegataki Falls is a stunning waterfall formed by the ancient eruptions of the Aso volcano. From mid to late November, the area bursts into a vibrant tapestry of colors as towering maple and beech trees transform the secluded forest gorge into a lively, colorful paradise.

The most striking feature here is the legendary "Urami no Taki," where you can walk behind the waterfall. Standing inside the expansive natural rock cave, you gaze out at the 10-meter drop and 20-meter wide crystal-clear water curtain, resembling a giant ribbon that beautifully contrasts with the golden foliage outside, showcasing an ethereal aesthetic.

The pathway to the base of the waterfall is equipped with comfortable wooden walkways and steps, but be cautious as the moisture keeps the ground slippery year-round. It’s highly recommended to wear professional non-slip sports shoes when visiting for the autumn leaves. To protect the delicate natural landscape, entry to the area requires a complete online reservation. Early morning visits on weekdays offer the best chance to capture mirror-like scenery.

仙巌園.count: numerous maple trees and abundant autumn leaves
仙巌園.fee: 大人 1,000 円（尚古集成館含）
仙巌園.address: 鹿兒島縣鹿兒島市吉野町9700-1
仙巌園.access_train: Take a bus from JR Kagoshima Chuo Station for about 30 minutes to "Sengan-en Mae."
仙巌園.access_car: About a 20-minute drive from Kyushu Expressway "Kagoshima Kita IC."
仙巌園.tagline: Discover the stunning beauty of Sengan-en, where nature and history unite against the backdrop of Sakurajima volcano.
仙巌園.desc:
Sengan-en, also known as Iso Teien, is a magnificent strolling garden created by the Satsuma lord Shimazu Mitsuhisa, designated as a national scenic spot. From late November to early December each year, the garden's hundreds of maple trees burst into vibrant autumn colors, adding a classical elegance and lively spirit to this historic site.

The most breathtaking view here features the "smoking Sakurajima volcano, Kinko Bay, and the autumn foliage in a grand composition." Standing in the spacious central garden plaza, visitors can capture the majestic silhouette of Sakurajima, the tranquil pond, and the vivid red maple leaves swaying by the water's edge, creating a scene that is both grand and harmonious.

The garden's terrain is mostly flat, with well-maintained stone pathways that are very friendly for seniors and strollers. The beautifully preserved wooden interiors of the Shimazu family's "Goten" mansion are highly recommended for a visit. Early mornings on weekdays are the best time to go, as the crowds are minimal, making it perfect for capturing the historic city in a flawless reflection.

首里城公園.types: Fukugi, Banyan trees (Okinawa features autumn flowers and evergreens)
首里城公園.count: Subtropical vegetation, with little noticeable autumn foliage
首里城公園.address: 沖繩縣那霸市首里金城町1-2
首里城公園.access_train: Approximately a 15-minute walk from Shuri Station on the Okinawa Urban Monorail (Yui Rail)
首里城公園.access_car: About a 30-minute drive from Naha Airport
首里城公園.tagline: Experience the majestic Ryukyu Kingdom's heritage at Shurijo Castle Park, where history meets vibrant autumn hues.
首里城公園.desc:
Shurijo Castle Park is a magnificent palace ruin that has preserved the rich history of the Ryukyu Kingdom for centuries, recognized as a UNESCO World Heritage site. Thanks to Okinawa's warm climate, the blooming season here is notably late, with the autumn foliage arriving as late as early December, adding vibrant colors to the grand red walls of the castle.

The park's core feature is the perfect blend of the iconic Shureimon Gate, the imposing castle walls, and a variety of traditional flowers. The rustic wooden gate, styled in Tang architecture, and the stone-paved streets are surrounded by exquisite local hydrangeas and Western varieties, creating a unique exotic atmosphere that complements the distant harbor.

The internal layout of the palace is well-planned, with most visitor pathways featuring smooth walkways and escalators, despite some steep stone steps and sloped walls. The site also includes an air-conditioned cultural exhibition hall. Visiting in the morning allows you to capture the rich textures of the old buildings, creating photographs filled with stories.

大雪山.count: The largest mountainous area for Japan's earliest autumn leaves
大雪山.fee: 免費（纜車成人往返 3,200 円）
大雪山.address: 北海道上川郡東川町旭岳溫泉
大雪山.access_train: About 90 minutes by bus "Idayu-go" from JR Asahikawa Station
大雪山.access_car: About 1 hour from Dōō Expressway Asahikawa North IC
大雪山.desc:
Daisetsuzan National Park is Japan's largest national park, renowned for being the first in the country to welcome autumn leaves. Due to its high altitude and latitude, alpine plants begin to change color in late August, and by early to mid-September, the entire mountain is blanketed in rich gradients of red and yellow.

The visual highlight is the richly layered "three-dimensional colorful carpet." Travelers can take the "Kurodake Ropeway" or "Asahidake Ropeway" to soar above, gazing down at a vast natural palette created by the intertwining of mountain plants like rowan and birch with lush green coniferous forests, delivering a stunning visual impact.

As the frontline of Japan's autumn foliage, temperatures at the summit can approach freezing by mid-September. Be sure to wear a professional mountain jacket that provides wind resistance and warmth when coming to enjoy the maple leaves. Mountain weather can change rapidly, so it's highly recommended to choose a clear morning with good visibility for your hike to capture the most breathtaking views of the majestic mountain scenery.
大雪山.tagline: Experience Japan's earliest autumn in the majestic peaks, surrounded by vibrant coniferous forests and fiery red tundra.

中禪寺湖.desc:
Chūzenji Lake, nestled within Nikkō National Park, is a natural dam lake sitting at an elevation of 1,269 meters. Every year from mid to late October, the maple and water birch trees surrounding the expansive lakeshore reach their peak foliage, transforming this historic summer retreat into a vibrant tapestry of colors.

The most iconic view is from the 'Hanyutsusan Observatory,' where visitors can capture the stunning sight of 'Hachijō Island,' resembling a giant dragon stretching into the lake, framed by the majestic Mount Nantai and the deep blue waters. Additionally, the autumn leaves along the nearby Kegon Falls are a must-see photo opportunity.

Due to severe traffic congestion on the 'Iroha Slope' during Nikkō's leaf season, it's highly recommended to set out early on weekdays before dawn. The mountaintop can be quite windy and cool, so be sure to bring a lightweight windbreaker for your outdoor adventures. Clear mornings provide the best chance to capture perfect reflections on the lake and the colorful foliage.
中禪寺湖.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of Nikkō's autumn leaves at the foot of Mount Nantai.
中禪寺湖.fee: 免費（中禪寺湖周邊無入場費；華嚴瀑布展望台大人 570 円）
中禪寺湖.access_train: Take a bus from Tōbu Nikkō Station or JR Nikkō Station towards Chūzenji Onsen, approximately 45 minutes.
中禪寺湖.access_car: About 20 minutes from the 'Kiyotaki IC' on the Nikkō Utsunomiya Road; expect traffic on Iroha Slope during leaf season, so early morning departures on weekdays are advised.
